En: Lieutenant Kitsuragi lies in the hospital, recovering. Barely. Are you really done here, he would ask you? Are you done with the investigation?
Zh: 曷城警督躺在医院里，正在恢复。很是勉强。他会问你，你真的做完所有工作了吗？调查完成了吗？

En: Yeah... I'm not. [Leave.]
Zh: 是啊……还没有。[离开。]

En: You reach the jetty and climb out of the skiff.
Zh: 你抵达码头，爬出小艇。

En: The skiff is swaying on the waves by the dock.
Zh: 码头旁的小艇在波浪中摇摆。

En: The skiff rocks gently under your weight as you get in. The ride back is uneventful and quiet.
Zh: 你踏上小艇，它在你的重量下轻柔地摇晃。回去的路上风平浪静。

En: But for the sound of conversation on the water. There is someone inland, waiting for you.
Zh: 只是水面上传来了对话声。有人在内陆，在等你。

En: Two men and a woman stand on the concrete square of a nameless village, looking at a small yellow boat as it draws closer. The sea is calm.
Zh: 两个男人和一个女人站在无名村庄的混凝土广场，看着一艘黄色小船越靠越近。大海十分平静。

En: Let's return to the mainland.
Zh: “我们返回内陆吧。”

En: Return to the mainland.
Zh: 返回内陆。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: You climb into the boat and start the engine.
Zh: 你爬上船，启动引擎。

En: Are you sure? There are things we still need to do here... He looks at the ruined flak tower on the cliffside.
Zh: “你确定吗？我们在这里还有事没做完……”他看着悬崖边废弃的高射炮塔。

En: Are you sure? There's shit we need to do, sheriff... He looks at the ruined flak tower on the cliffside.
Zh: “你确定吗？我们还有些破事要处理呢，警长……”他看着悬崖边废弃的高射炮塔。

En: You're right. Let's get to it. [Leave.]
Zh: “你说得对。我们开始吧。”[离开。]

En: Let's. We're done here, he says, adjusting his glasses as he looks out over the water.
Zh: “走吧。这里的事已经做完了，”他说着，调整了一下眼镜，望向水面。

En: Oh yeah. He crosses his arms and nods to you with a self-satisfied smirk. "We did good shit here, detective."
Zh: “哦耶。”他交叉双臂，朝你点点头，露出一个自鸣得意的傻笑。“我们在这里干得不错，警探。”

En: You're right. Let's get to it. [Leave.]
Zh: “你说得对。我们开始吧。”[离开。]

En: Fuckin'... May bells everywhere.
Zh: “妈的……到处都是铃兰。”

En: Empty... Gotta look elsewhere, piggo.
Zh: “空的……去其他地方看看吧，猪头。”

En: Got the fucking fuel, yeah.
Zh: “他妈的，是有燃料，哦耶。”

En: Iosef Lilianovich Dros, you are under arrest for the murder of the mercenary leader, Kortenaer. You will be taken to the nearest precinct holding area for preliminary investigation.
Zh: “伊索夫·利连诺维奇·德罗斯，你因为谋杀雇佣兵首领科顿艾尔而被逮捕了。你将会被送往最近的分局等候区，接受初步调查。”

En: You will be provided legal counsel within one week, and must face court in 44 days.
Zh: “我们将会在一周内为你指派法律顾问，而且必须在44天内出庭。”

En: Your Wayfarer rights have been suspended. Information provided to the officer on the scene will be used against you by the prosecution.
Zh: “你的旅人权被暂停了。现场警员提供的信息将会成为控方的呈堂证供。”

En: Does the boat have room for three?
Zh: “船的空间够塞下三个人的吗？”

En: *You* could come back for me. Once you've taken him to the Precinct.
Zh: “*你*可以回来接我。等你把他带回分局之后。”

En: Maybe I can just ask that Net Picker woman to watch him?
Zh: “也许我可以让拾网人帮忙看着他？”

En: Maybe I can just ask Lilienne to watch him?
Zh: “也许我可以让莉莉恩帮忙看着他？”

En: Maybe you're right...
Zh: “也许你是对的……”

En: We could all fit in the little boat?
Zh: “我们都能塞进那条小船？”

En: *But* what? There's something I'm not getting, right?
Zh: “*但是*什么？有些事情我没弄明白，对吗？”

En: I can't let a kid go and take a murder suspect to the station.
Zh: “我不能让一个小孩带着杀人嫌疑犯去警局吧。”

En: Maybe you're right...
Zh: “也许你是对的……”

En: Yeah, actually, that will look bad.
Zh: “是啊，其实，那样看起来应该挺糟糕的。”

En: Yeah -- no. He shakes his head. "Only two are gonna fit in that fucker. I guess *you* could take him back, but..." He squints, realizing something.
Zh: “哦耶——不行。”他摇摇头。“那破玩意里只能塞下两个人。我猜*你*可以把他带回去，不过……”他眯起眼睛，突然意识到了什么。

En: The kid looks at you blankly. Then at the old man. "You fuckin' with me? Coz I can. I *can* take him."
Zh: 男孩茫然地看着你。然后是那个老人。“你他妈看不起我啊？我当然可以啊。我*可以*带他走。”

En: What is this... farce? He looks around, with some desperation. "This a fucking farce, I can't..."
Zh: “这是什么……闹剧吗？”他环顾四周，带着一种奇怪的绝望。“这是一场该死的闹剧，我不能……”

En: Wait! But then -- who watches over him while you're coming back?
Zh: 等等！但是有一个问题啊——你回来的路上谁看着他呢？

En: But then who watches him -- there -- while I come back for you?
Zh: “但是，谁看着他呢——在那边——在我回来接你的时候？”

En: He squints harder. "Fuck, it's the three piggies and a boat problem..."
Zh: 他白眼都要翻上天了。“妈的，这他妈是三头猪和一条船过河的问题……”

En: Cold. From the East.
Zh: 很冷。来自东边。

En: Watch this *killer*? The kid shakes his head and points to him.
Zh: “看着这个*杀人犯*？”他指着老人，摇摇头。

En: This fucking world... He stares at something -- who knows what -- in the dust. "This world... what is this?"
Zh: “这个操蛋的世界……”他凝视着什么——谁知道是什么——在灰尘里。“这个世界……这是什么？”

En: Uhm -- how about the Cuno comes back for *you*?
Zh: “呃——不如坤诺回来接*你*怎么样？”

En: Below the confusion and rage -- a fit of jamais vu, like yours? The thought passes, more pressing matters take its place...
Zh: 在困惑和愤怒之下——突发的旧事如新症，跟你一样？这个想法一闪而过，更紧迫的事情占据了它的位置……

En: Your skin is crawling suddenly.
Zh: 你皮肤上的汗毛突然立了起来。

En: Yeah, actually -- no. He scratches his head. "It would go down."
Zh: “是啊，其实——不行。”他挠挠头。“它会沉的。”

En: This *is* a problem, the kid says. "Cuno's literally had this problem. In a fuckin' textbook. When he went to *kiddie school*. This is that three piggies and a boat shit..."
Zh: “这*的确*是个问题，”男孩说到。“坤诺真的遇到过这种问题。在一个该死的‘课本’里。当他去上什么狗屁*小学校*的时候。三只猪头和一条船什么的……”

En: His eyes shoot wide open. "Cuno understands what's wrong. Who watches this fuckin' killer in the village, while you come back for me?"
Zh: 他的眼睛睁得大大的。“坤诺知道哪里出问题了。你回来接我的时候，村子里谁能看着这个该死的杀人犯呢？”

En: *I* could come back for you. Once I've taken him to the Precinct.
Zh: “*我*可以回来接你。等我把他带回分局之后。”

En: This is not a problem.
Zh: “这不是个问题。”

En: There's something you're not getting here that he has just realized.
Zh: 他刚刚意识到，有些问题你没弄明白。

En: The words come to you, clear as day.
Zh: 你想到那些话了，一清二楚。

En: Lilienne. You could ask her maybe?
Zh: 莉莉恩。也许你可以找她帮忙？

En: Lilienne. You could ask her.
Zh: 莉莉恩。你可以找她帮忙。

En: Something is happening. Stop.
Zh: 有些事正在发生。停住。

En: This fucking world... The old man repeats. "What are you *talking* about? Is this..." His voice drowns out in a sudden gush of wind. "Us..."
Zh: “这个操蛋的世界……”老人又打断了你们。“你在*说*什么呢？这是……”他的声音突然被一阵狂风吞没了。“我们……”

En: This fucking world... The old man interferes again. "What are you *talking* about? Is this..." His voice drowns out in a sudden gush of wind. "Us..."
Zh: “这个操蛋的世界……”老人又打断了你们。“你在*说*什么呢？这是……”他的声音突然被一阵狂风吞没了。“我们……”

En: Stop. Something is happening.
Zh: 有些事正在发生。停住。

En: He seems oblivious to the question. The waves slowly lap against the seafort to the south...
Zh: 他似乎没有注意这个问题。海浪慢慢地拍打着南边的海堡……

En: No. It's not that.
Zh: 不。不是这么回事。

En: Th-the... The old man stutters. Then another sigh.
Zh: “那-那个……”老人结结巴巴。然后又是一声叹息。

En: No. It's not that.
Zh: 不。不是这么回事。

En: Th-the... The old man stutters. Then another sigh.
Zh: “那-那个……”老人结结巴巴。然后又是一声叹息。

En: No. Forget it. Inspect the man.
Zh: “不。忘了吧。”查看那个老人。

En: He does *not* look hormonal anymore. Motionless. His plastic cape flaps in a sudden gust of wind.
Zh: 他看起来*不再*激动了。一动不动。一阵海风吹来，他的塑料斗篷上下飞舞。

En: Try something else? We got him back for a moment.
Zh: “试试别的？我们刚才唤回了他一下。”

En: It's right there, can you see it? (Point.)
Zh: “它就在那里，你看不见吗？”（指过去。）

En: N-n... He doesn't finish the sentence. One letter is all he can produce. His hands are numb and he stares at them.
Zh: “不……”他的话没有说完。他只能发出一个单音节。他的手麻木了，眼睛直勾勾地盯着它们。

En: No, listen! Listen now!
Zh: 不，听！快听！

En: Nothing -- 'nothing' is what he tried to say.
Zh: 没什么——他是打算说‘没什么’。

En: There is nothing. Only the reeds for him. The reeds and devastation.
Zh: 什么也没有。对他来说只有芦苇。芦苇和荒芜。

En: No one would believe you without it.
Zh: 没有它，没人会相信你的。

En: You will have to be very convincing and very *sober* to make them believe, sire.
Zh: 大人，要想让他们相信，您必须非常有说服力，非常*清醒*。

En: The old man looks at his rifle in the sand, then up at you -- a little startled for some reason...
Zh: 老人看着沙地里的步枪，然后抬头看着你——莫名有些惊慌……

En: The old man looks at his rifle in your hands -- a little startled for some reason...
Zh: 老人看着他的步枪握在你手里——莫名有些惊慌……

En: Officer -- are we sure? His voice is lowered. "We could maybe get more."
Zh: “警官——你确定吗？”他压低了声音。“也许我们还能挖出更多信息。”

En: ... is what you plan to say. Before you can get past 'you're under', the lieutenant interrupts you:
Zh: ……你计划这么说。在你还没来得及说出‘你被……’之前，警督就打断了你：

En: Officer... The lieutenant interrupts you at *under*. His voice is hushed. "Are we sure? We could maybe get more..."
Zh: “警官，”在你说到*逮*字的时候，警督打断了你。他低声说到。“你确定吗？也许我们还能挖出更多信息……”

En: (Nod.) "We've been thorough enough."
Zh: （点点头。）“我们已经问的很彻底了。”

En: (Turn to the suspect.) "One more thing, Mr. Dros..."
Zh: （转向嫌疑人。）“还有一件事，德罗斯先生……”

En: You've startled him... maybe it was the silence? He quickly gathers himself. Still -- what could he be afraid of?
Zh: 你吓到他了……也许是因为沉默？他很快振作了起来。不过——他可能会怕谁呢？

En: He nods back and turns to the man sitting on the log. "Iosef Lilianovich Dros, you are under arrest for the murder of Ellis Kortenaer. You will be taken to the nearest precinct holding area for preliminary investigation."
Zh: 他点头回应，转向坐在木块上的男人。“伊索夫·利连诺维奇·德罗斯，你因为谋杀埃利斯·科顿艾尔而被逮捕了。你将会被送往最近的分局等候区，接受初步调查。”

En: He nods back and turns to the man sitting on the log. "Iosef Lilianovich Dros, you are under arrest for the murder of the Krenel colonel, Lely. You will be taken to the nearest precinct holding area for preliminary investigation."
Zh: 他点头回应，转向坐在木块上的男人。“伊索夫·利连诺维奇·德罗斯，你因为谋杀科尔奈上校——莱利而被逮捕了。你将会被送往最近的分局等候区，接受初步调查。”

En: He nods back and turns to the man sitting on the log. "Iosef Lilianovich Dros, you are under arrest for the murder of the Krenel colonel. You will be taken to the nearest precinct holding area for preliminary investigation."
Zh: 他点头回应，转向坐在木块上的男人。“伊索夫·利连诺维奇·德罗斯，你因为谋杀科尔奈上校而被逮捕了。你将会被送往最近的分局等候区，接受初步调查。”

En: Your Wayfarer rights have been suspended. Information provided to the officers on the scene will be used against you by the prosecution. You will be given legal counsel within one week, and must face court in 44 days -- do you understand?
Zh: “你的旅人权被暂停了。现场警员提供的信息将会成为控方的呈堂证供。我们将会在一周内为你指派法律顾问，而且必须在44天内出庭——你明白了吗？”

En: The wind picks up. The silence on the water is broken all around you, little shivers of waves appear. The lieutenant continues, like an incantation...
Zh: 起风了。周围水面的寂静被打破了，海浪微微颤抖。警督继续念叨着，就像一串咒语……

En: Do you understand?
Zh: “你明白了吗？”

En: What?! The old man's eyes fill with sudden, unexpected terror at the words: "But you said I would be taken to the..."
Zh: “什么？！”因为这些话语，老人的眼睛里突然闪现出意料之外的恐惧。“但你不是说我会被送到……”

En: This terror is the sum of all the uncontrollable movements and mood swings he's been exhibiting.
Zh: 这种恐惧是所有无法控制的动作，还有他表现出来的情绪波动的总和。

En: The sum of all the erratic movements, fidgeting and mood swings he's been exhibiting...
Zh: 所有古怪动作，烦躁，还有他表现出来的情绪波动的总和……

En: But...
Kim, he's afraid...
Zh: “金，他在害怕……”

En: Do you understand, sir?
Zh: “你明白了吗，先生？”

En: No, I don't want to! I have to stay here. He looks at the reeds, eyes submerged in growing terror.
Zh: “不，我不想这样！我必须待在这里。”他看着芦苇丛，眼里的恐惧越积越多。

En: He's sweating. Beads are forming on his forehead.
Zh: 他在流汗，额头上出现了豆大的汗珠。

En: Pupils are dilated too, eyes getting blacker and blacker.
Zh: 瞳孔也在扩张，眼珠变得越来越黑。

En: He's not afraid of jail. He's afraid of something else.
Zh: 他不害怕监狱。他在害怕别的。

En: Like parting from a loved one.
Zh: 就像跟爱人分离。

En: Your confirmation is not required, sir. The lieutenant turns to you. "Now -- on to the boat..."
Zh: “不需要你的确认，先生。”警督转向你。“现在——上船吧……”

En: First say: "Does it have room for *three*?"
Zh: 首先：“空间够*三个人*吗？”

En: *You* could come back for me. Once you've taken him to the Precinct.
Zh: “*你*可以回来接我。等你把他带回分局之后。”

En: Wait...
Zh: 等等……

En: Inland Empire warned.
Zh: 本心在警告。

En: Jamais vu...
Zh: 旧事如新……

En: Not really. He shakes his head. "We could escort him to the pier, then either one of us can take him inland while the other stays here, but..."
Zh: “不见得。”他摇摇头。“我们可以把他护送到码头，然后我们其中一个把他带回内陆，另一个在这里等着，不过……”

En: What is this... farce? He looks around, with strange desperation. "This a fucking farce, I can't..."
Zh: “这是什么……闹剧吗？”他环顾四周，带着一种奇怪的绝望。“这是一场该死的闹剧，我不能……”

En: But then -- who watches him, while you're coming back here?
Zh: 不过——你回来这里的时候，谁看着他呢？

En: Who watches him there while I come back for you?
Zh: “我回来找你的时候，谁看着他呢？”

En: You come back for me? How about I go and send a boat back for you.
Zh: “你回来找我？不如我去，然后派船回来接你怎么样？”

En: Maybe I can just ask that Net Picker to watch him?
Zh: “也许我可以让拾网人帮忙看着他？”

En: Maybe I can just ask Lilienne to watch him?
Zh: “也许我可以让莉莉恩帮忙看着他？”

En: This is no harmless old man. The lieutenant shakes his head.
Zh: “他不是一个人畜无害的老人。”警督摇摇头。

En: This fucking world... He stares at something -- who knows what -- in the dust. "This world... what is this?"
Zh: “这个操蛋的世界……”他凝视着什么——谁知道是什么——在灰尘里。“这个世界……这是什么？”

En: Below the confusion and rage -- a fit of jamais vu, like yours? The thought passes, more pressing matters take its place...
Zh: 在困惑和愤怒之下——突发的旧事如新症，跟你一样？这个想法一闪而过，更紧迫的事情占据了它的位置……

En: But what?
Zh: “但是什么？”

En: The lieutenant shakes his head, frustrated. "Who watches over him there while I come back for you?"
Zh: 警督挫败地摇摇头。“我回来找你的时候，那里谁能看着他呢？”

En: This is not a problem.
Zh: “这不是个问题。”

En: There's something you're not getting here that he's realizing.
Zh: 他刚刚意识到，有些问题你没弄明白。

En: This is a problem, the lieutenant says. "Who watches over him while I come back for you?"
Zh: “这是个问题，”警督说到。“我回来找你的时候，谁看着他呢？”

En: Something is happening. Stop.
Zh: 有些事正在发生。停住。

En: Your skin is crawling suddenly.
Zh: 你皮肤上的汗毛突然立了起来。

En: *I* could come back for you. Once I've taken him to the Precinct.
Zh: “*我*可以回来找你。等我把他带到分局之后。”

En: ... is what you want to say. You can barely get to 'you're under...' when you suddenly get weary...
Zh: ……你想要这么说。在你还没来得及说出‘你被’，突然感到一阵厌倦……

En: Did you get *everything*, detective?
Zh: 你真的把*一切*都搞清楚了吗，警探？

En: It may be too late later, the circumstances change, are you sure we're done here?
Zh: 之后也许就太迟了，情况变了，你确定这里已经完事了？

En: ... is what you want to say. You get to 'you're under...' when a warning rings in the back of your head: did you get everything?
Zh: ……你想要这么说。你刚准备说出‘你被逮……’，突然脑后传来一阵警告的声音：你真把一切都搞清楚了吗？

En: ... is what you want to say, but wait god damn it! With the words 'you're under...' still stuck in your throat you think: what if I *missed* something?
Zh: ……你想要这么说，但是，该死的，等等！‘你被逮……’还卡在你的喉咙里，你突然想到：如果我*错过*了什么呢？

En: (Turn to the suspect.) "One more thing, Mr. Dros..."
Zh: （转向嫌疑人。）“还有一件事，德罗斯先生……”

En: (No, I got everything I need, say:) "Iosef Lilianovich Dros -- you are under arrest for the murder of Ellis Kortenaer."
Zh: （不，我需要的都有了，说吧：）“伊索夫·利连诺维奇·德罗斯，你因为谋杀埃利斯·科顿艾尔而被逮捕了。”

En: (No, I got everything I need, say:) "Iosef Lilianovich Dros -- you are under arrest for the murder of Lely, the mercenary."
Zh: （不，我需要的都有了，说吧：）“伊索夫·利连诺维奇·德罗斯，你因为谋杀雇佣兵莱利而被逮捕了。”

En: (No, I got everything I need, say:) "Iosef Lilianovich Dros -- you are under arrest for the murder of the Krenel colonel."
Zh: （不，我需要的都有了，说吧：）“伊索夫·利连诺维奇·德罗斯，你因为谋杀科奈尔上校而被逮捕了。”

En: Huh? He leans closer with a strange expression on his face.
Zh: “哈？”他靠近了一些，脸上的表情很奇怪。

En: (Say:) "You will be taken to the nearest precinct holding area for preliminary investigation."
Zh: （说到：）“你将会被送往最近的分局等候区，接受初步调查。”

En: The words feel heavy, like they're coming from the bottom of you. You've said them half a thousand times...
Zh: 这些话语沉甸甸的，好像来源于你的心底。你已经说过500多次了……

En: But... His eyes fill with sudden terror: "You said I would be taken to the, uhm, the..."
Zh: “但是……”他的眼睛里满是突如其来的恐惧：“但你不是说我会被送到……呃，那个……”

En: This terror, the sum of all the uncontrollable movements and mood swings he's been exhibiting...
Zh: 这种恐惧，是所有无法控制的动作，还有他表现出来的情绪波动的总和……

En: The wind picks up. The silence on the water is broken, little shivers of waves appear...
Zh: 起风了。周围水面的寂静被打破了，海浪微微颤抖。警督继续念叨着，就像一串咒语……

En: The sum of all the erratic, involuntary movements and mood swings he's been exhibiting...
Zh: 所有古怪动作，烦躁，还有他表现出来的情绪波动的总和……

En: (Say:) "Your Wayfarer rights have been suspended. Information provided to the officer on the scene will be used against you by the prosecution."
Zh: （说到：）“你的旅人权被暂停了。现场警员提供的信息将会成为控方的呈堂证供。”

En: You will be provided legal counsel within one week, and must face court in 44 days -- do you understand?
Zh: “我们将会在一周内为你指派法律顾问，而且必须在44天内出庭——你明白了吗？”

En: Do you understand?
Zh: “你明白了吗？”

En: I... He looks around, confused.
Zh: “我……”他环顾四周，样子很困惑。

En: Do you understand, sir?
Zh: “你明白了吗，先生？”

En: You're afraid...
Zh: “你在害怕……”

En: What if I don't want to? What if I want to stay here? He looks at you, eyes submerged in terror.
Zh: “如果我不想呢？如果我想待在这里呢？”他看着你，眼里满是恐惧。

En: He is a brave man -- why is he unravelling?
Zh: 他是个勇敢的男人——为什么会崩溃呢？

En: How is this brave man unravelling?
Zh: 这个勇敢的男人为什么会崩溃呢？

En: Look, he's sweating. Beads are forming on his forehead.
Zh: 看，他在流汗，额头上出现了豆大的汗珠。

En: Pupils are dilated too, eyes getting blacker and blacker...
Zh: 瞳孔也在扩张，眼珠变得越来越黑……

En: He's not afraid of jail. He's afraid of something else...
Zh: 他不害怕监狱。他在害怕别的……

En: Like parting from a loved one.
Zh: 就像跟爱人分离。

En: It feels like a *spell*.
Zh: 感觉就像是一个*咒语*。

En: Now that you say them, the words feel like tin, heavy, they're coming from the bottom of you. You've said them half a thousand times...
Zh: 你说出这些话语，感觉像锡制的，沉甸甸，好像来源于你的心底。你已经说过几百次了……

En: Okay, the lieutenant nods. "How about *I* take him to the Precinct and you wait on the island?"
Zh: “好吧，”警督点点头。“不如*我*把他送去分局，你在岛上等着呢？”

En: No, no, it would take a whole day on this island. You go and transport the prisoner, I'll be here. I can escort him to the pier with you.
Zh: “不不，在这座岛上要花上一整天时间的。你去押送犯人，我在这里等着。我可以跟你一起护送他到码头。”

En: No need to be polite, I'll do it.
Zh: “没必要客气，我来吧。”

En: Maybe you're right...
Zh: “也许你是对的……”

En: I'll do it, let's not get into this.
Zh: “我来吧，还是别扯个不停了。”

En: Maybe you're right, okay...
Zh: “也许你是对的，好吧……”

En: This world... The old man repeats. "What are you *talking* about? Is this..." His voice drowns out in a sudden gush of wind. "Us..."
Zh: “这个世界……”老人重复到。“你们在*说*什么呢？这是……”他的声音突然被一阵狂风吞没了。“我们……”

En: This world... The old man interferes again. "What are you *talking* about? Is this..." His voice drowns out in a sudden gush of wind. "Us..."
Zh: “这个世界……”老人又打断了你们。“你们在*说*什么呢？这是……”他的声音突然被一阵狂风吞没了。“我们……”

En: The wind is cold. From the east.
Zh: 从东边吹来的风，很冷。

En: There it is again, to your north, as it has been since you came to the coast -- the reeds whisper, stalks rubbing against each other. But then, in the middle of it...
Zh: 又来了，在你北边，自从你来到这个海岸，它就一直在那里——芦苇在低语，茎杆互相摩擦。不过，在那中间……

En: You thought you heard something but it's just the reeds...
Zh: 你觉得自己听见了某种声音，不过那里只有芦苇……

En: What?
Zh: 什么？

En: Something completely different. It sounds like a bow, very slowly being drawn against the strings of a violin. A very small violin made of reeds and rushes.
Zh: 某种完全不同的东西。听起来像一支琴弓，非常缓慢地在小提琴的弦上拉动着。一个用芦苇和蒲草做成的小提琴。

En: Shush, Kim! Do you hear that?
Zh: “嘘，金！你听见了吗？”

En: Okay, so you're saying we can all fit in there?
Zh: “好吧，所以你是说我们都能塞进去？”

En: ... and then it's gone. Drowned out by the lieutenant's voice -- "Maybe there *is* room for three on the boat..."
Zh: ……然后就没了。淹没在警督的声音里——“也许船上*有*位置够我们三个人……”

En: ... and then it's gone. Drowned out by the lieutenant's voice -- "Maybe there is room for three on the boat..."
Zh: ……然后就没了。淹没在警督的声音里——“也许船上有位置够我们三个人……”

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: What are you *talking* about? Is this... The old man's voice drowns in a sudden gust of wind. "...really us..."
Zh: “你们在*说*什么？这是……”老人的声音突然被一阵狂风吞没了。“……真的，我们……”

En: Your skin crawls.
Zh: 你皮肤上的汗毛竖了起来。

En: We should try first. There is a prisoner transfer protocol that requires two...
Zh: “我们应该先试一下。囚犯押送协议说要求两个人……”

En: Maybe we could all fit in there... the lieutenant says.
Zh: “也许我们都能塞进去……”警督说到。

En: Actually, no... He scratches his head.
Zh: “事实上，不行……”他挠挠头。

En: We could all fit in the little boat?
Zh: “我们都能塞进那条小船？”

En: First, you need to come with me to the boat.
Zh: “首先，你需要跟我一起到船那边去。”

En: Let's go. I don't have handcuffs, so you walk in front of me.
Zh: “我们走吧。我没有手铐，所以你在我前面走。”

En: Can you stay here while I go inland and find you transport?
Zh: “你能待在这里吗，我回内陆找人来押送你？”

En: Wait...
Zh: 等等……

En: Col Do Ma Ma Daqua
Zh: 哈库多玛达塔

En: Inland Empire warned.
Zh: 本心在警告。

En: There it is again, to your north, as it has been since you came to the coast -- the reeds whisper, stalks rubbing against each other. But then, in the middle of it...
Zh: 又来了，在你北边，自从你来到这个海岸，它就一直在那里——芦苇在低语，茎杆互相摩擦。不过，在那中间……

En: Your skin crawls.
Zh: 你皮肤上的汗毛竖了起来。

En: What?
Zh: 什么？

En: Mr. Dros, time to go.
Zh: “德罗斯先生，该走了。”

En: Something completely different. It sounds like a bow, very slowly being drawn against the strings of a violin. A very small violin made of reeds and rushes.
Zh: 某种完全不同的东西。听起来像一支琴弓，非常缓慢地在小提琴的弦上拉动着。一个用芦苇和蒲草做成的小提琴。

En: A farce -- this world. Is this... is this us... His voice drowns out in a sudden gust of wind.
Zh: “一场闹剧——这个世界。这是……这是我们……”他的声音突然被一阵狂风吞没了。

En: You thought you heard something, but it's just the reeds. Now -- how do we do this?
Zh: 你觉得自己听见了某种声音，不过那里只有芦苇。现在——我们该怎么做呢？

En: Mr. Dros, do you hear that?
Zh: “德罗斯先生，你听见了吗？”

En: I can't, my rubber dinghy... He points to the broken tyre. "My tyre is broken, it won't inflate."
Zh: “我不行，我的橡皮艇……”他指向破掉的轮胎。“我的轮胎破了，充不了气。”

En: He's regressing, fast.
Zh: 他在退化，很快。

En: He's been stuck on the island after the tyre broke...
Zh: 轮胎破了之后，他一直被困在这座岛上……

En: What a farce. What are you *talking* about -- handcuffs? Is this... The old man's voice drowns in a sudden gush of wind.
Zh: “真是一场闹剧。你在*说*什么呢——手铐？这是……”老人的声音突然被一阵狂风吞没了。

En: What a farce. What are you *talking* about -- inland? Is this... The old man's voice drowns in a sudden gush of wind.
Zh: “真是一场闹剧。你在*说*什么呢——内陆？这是……”老人的声音突然被一阵狂风吞没了。

En: Cold. From the East.
Zh: 很冷。来自东边。

En: Your skin crawls.
Zh: 你皮肤上的汗毛竖了起来。

En: But that would mean...
Zh: 但是那就意味着……

En: But that means...
Zh: 但那意味着……

En: It's really there. Spinning slowly -- in absolute silence -- its limbs long and slender.
Zh: 它真的在那里。慢慢地旋转——在绝对的沉默中——它的节肢修长而纤细。

En: It's really there. Spinning slowly -- in absolute silence -- its limbs long and slender.
Zh: 它真的在那里。慢慢地旋转——在绝对的沉默中——它的节肢修长而纤细。

En: My wife said I'm insane too. That's why we can't...
Zh: “我妻子也说过我疯了。所以我们不能……”

En: Guess you have, comrade. You hear the old man's voice in the dark. "I don't see anything there -- does this mean I'm not under arrest?"
Zh: “我猜你就是，同伴。”你听到黑暗中传来老人的声音。“我什么也没看见——这是不是意味着我没有被逮捕？”

En: Guess you have, droite. You hear the old man's voice in the dark. "I don't see anything there -- does this mean I'm not under arrest?"
Zh: “我猜你就是，右翼分子。”黑暗中传来老人的声音。“我什么也没看见——这是不是意味着我没有被逮捕？”

En: You feel your legs shaking under you and your gun hand move to your holster -- to grab the gun...
Zh: 你感觉双腿在身下颤抖，惯用握枪的那只手本能地伸向枪套——去抓手枪……

En: A delicate tangle of arms and legs unfolds from the reeds, limb by limb -- to then just stand there, moving its scythe-like arms in ghostly silence.
Zh: 胳膊和腿精妙地纠缠在一起，在芦苇丛中慢慢展开节肢，一个接着一个——然后站在那里，在可怕的沉默中挥舞着镰刀样的手臂。

En: Be very, *very* careful, the lieutenant whispers, then takes a step toward the giant arthropod...
Zh: “要非常、*非常*小心，”警督低语到，然后朝着巨大的节肢动物靠近了一步……

En: ...phasmid! You open your eyes and there still is, spinning slowly -- in absolute  silence -- its limbs long and slender...
Zh: ……竹节虫！你睁开双眼，它就在那里，慢慢地旋转——在绝对的沉默中——它的节肢修长而纤细。

En: Four simple words -- thank god. If he can see, then you're not insane.
Zh: 四个简单的词语——谢天谢地。如果他能看见，那你就没有发疯。

En: If he can see it too, then you're not *totally* insane.
Zh: 如果他也看见了，那你没有*完全*发疯。

En: ...but Lieutenant Kitsuragi is not here. There is only the old straddler. He turns his black eyes to you and says: "Kim who? There's no Kim here either."
Zh: ……不过曷城警督不在这里。只有这个年迈的逃兵。他的黑眼睛转向你，说到：“金是谁？这里没有叫金的人。”

En: I can see it.
Zh: “我能看见。”

En: He's right. You've lost your mind.
Zh: 他是对的。你疯了。

En: There is only the old man -- and the giant stick insect spinning slowly -- in absolute silence -- it's long and thin limbs...
Zh: 只有这个老人——还有那只慢慢旋转的巨大竹节虫——在绝对的沉默中——它的节肢修长而纤细……

En: Beckoning you to come to it.
Zh: 它在召唤你。

En: You feel your legs shaking under you and your gun hand move to your holster -- but you don't have a gun...
Zh: 你感觉双腿在身下颤抖，惯用握枪的那只手本能地伸向枪套——但是你并没有枪……

En: The stick insect is over three metres tall. It looks straight at you with its tiny pinprick eyes and its grotesquely small head.
Zh: 竹节虫3米多高。它用针孔般的小眼睛直直地盯着你，还有它那小的出奇的脑袋。

En: Kim, can *you* see it?!
Zh: “金，*你*能看见它吗？！”

En: Cuno, can *you* see it?!
Zh: “坤诺，*你*能看见它吗？！”

En: Thank god. If he can see, then you're not insane.
Zh: 谢天谢地。如果他能看见，那你就没有发疯。

En: Blink.
Zh: 眨眨眼。

En: No, I can't be insane -- you're wrong.
Zh: “不，我没有发疯——你搞错了。”

En: But you *can*. You open your eyes and it's still there -- a giant stick insect, spinning slowly -- -- its limbs long and slender...
Zh: 但是你*可以*。你睁开双眼，它还在那里——一只巨大的竹节虫，慢慢地旋转——它的节肢修长而纤细……

En: It's still there -- an unfolding mechanism of reed-like chitin. Hovering in place.
Zh: 它还在那里——像一个展开的芦苇状壳质装置。

En: I can see it.
Zh: “我能看见。”

En: No! It's the cryptid!
Zh: 不！它是神秘动物！

En: You feel the lieutenant's hand on your back. And then you hear him say four words:
Zh: 你感觉警督把手搭在你的背后。然后你听见他说出了四个字：

En: What is THAT!?! (Point to it.)
Zh: “那是什么！？！”（指向它。）

En: What are you talking about? The old man looks at the reeds, then at you.
Zh: 老人看着芦苇，然后看着你：“你在说什么呢？”

En: No one would. You open your eyes and it's still there -- a giant stick insect, spinning slowly -- its limbs long and slender. Silent as death...
Zh: 没人可以。你睁开双眼，它还在那里——一只巨大的竹节虫，慢慢地旋转——它的节肢修长而纤细。死一般地沉寂……

En: No, it's the cryptid!
Zh: 不，它是神秘动物！

En: You feel your legs shaking under you and your gun hand rise instinctively...
Zh: 你感觉双腿在身下颤抖，惯用握枪的那只手本能举了起来……

En: I don't know what this means...
Zh: “我不知道这是什么意思……”

En: Tell me what you see, dammit, I can't make out one small thing in the reeds...
Zh: “告诉我你看见了什么，可恶，我在芦苇丛里什么都没看见……”

En: My god... maybe you *are*.
Zh: 天啦……也许你*是*呢。

En: No, it's the damn cryptid, the...
Zh: “不，这是只该死的神秘动物，那个……”

En: See what exactly?! A bug? He does not understand.
Zh: “到底看见什么了？！一只昆虫？”他没明白。

En: No. It can't be!
Zh: 不。不可能的！

En: You're high. Breathe in, you're just high!
Zh: 你很兴奋。快吸气，你只是太兴奋了！

En: Delirium! It's delirium from the booze!
Zh: 精神错乱！是因为饮酒导致的精神错乱！

En: It's too late, you quit drinking too late!
Zh: 太迟了，你戒酒太晚了！

En: Way too high. You open your eyes and it's still there -- a giant stick insect, spinning slowly -- its limbs long and slender. Silent as death...
Zh: 太兴奋了。你睁开双眼，它还在那里——一只巨大的竹节虫，慢慢地旋转——它的节肢修长而纤细。死一般地沉寂……

En: I've finally gone insane... (Put your head in your hands.)
Zh: “我最终还是疯了……”（用手抱住脑袋。）

En: There IS! I see it!
Zh: “有的！我看见它了！”

En: There IS! I see it!
Zh: “它在那里！我看见它了！”

En: Oh god, I'm too high...
Zh: “天啦，我太兴奋了……”

En: You're NOT insane.
Zh: 你没有发疯。

En: *Or* you're high.
Zh: *或者*你只是太兴奋了。

En: The giant stick insect!
Zh: “巨大的竹节虫！”

En: There's nothing there. He looks confused.
Zh: 他看起来很困惑。“那里什么都没有。”

En: A giant bug, watch out!
Zh: “一只巨大的昆虫，小心！”

En: I don't know what the hell that is...
Zh: “我不知道那是什么鬼东西……”

En: It's the cryptid!
Zh: 它是一只神秘动物！

En: ...be together. You open your eyes and it's still there -- a giant stick insect, spinning slowly -- its limbs long and slender....
Zh: ……在一起。你睁开双眼，它还在那里——一只巨大的竹节虫，慢慢地旋转——它的节肢修长而纤细……

En: S-s-s-see... He stares at the reeds, where your finger is pointing.
Zh: “看看看……”他凝视着芦苇丛，看着你指向的地方。

En: Can you see it *now*? (Point to the Phasmid)
Zh: “*现在*你能看见了吗？”（指着竹节虫）

En: Sir, how could you not *see* the phasmid?
Zh: “先生，你怎么可能*看*不见竹节虫呢？”

En: We found some things in the phasmid's nest, Mr. Dros.
Zh: “我们在竹节虫的巢穴里找到了一些东西，德罗斯先生。”

En: I found some things in the phasmid's nest, Mr. Dros.
Zh: “我在竹节虫的巢穴里找到了一些东西，德罗斯先生。”

En: Mr. Dros?
Zh: “德罗斯先生？”

En: The man does not respond -- he keeps staring, black eyes glazed over and bulging from their sockets, his gap toothed mouth shaking.
Zh: 男人没有回应——他一直盯着一个地方，目光呆滞，眼珠从眼窝里鼓了出来，嘴巴在颤抖。

En: Like an addict of some terrible substance.
Zh: 就像对某种可怕的物质上瘾了一样。

En: Snap your fingers under his nose.
Zh: 在他的鼻子下面打个响指。

En: Wave your hand in front of his eyes.
Zh: 在他的眼前挥挥手。

En: Touch his shoulder gently.
Zh: 轻轻地拍一下他的肩膀。

En: S-s-s-see... He stares at the reeds and falls silent.
Zh: “看看看……”他凝视着芦苇丛，陷入沉默。

En: Mr. Dros?
Zh: “德罗斯先生？”

En: I'll leave you be for now. [Leave.]
Zh: “我暂时先不打扰你了。”[离开。]

En: With fear and longing.
Zh: 带着恐惧和渴望。

En: A light shiver passes him -- followed by nothing. His hands are trembling and he breathes slowly.
Zh: 他的身体一阵轻微地颤抖——之后什么也没发生。他双手颤抖，呼吸平缓。

En: The plastic cape feels coarse. A light shiver passes the man -- other than that, no reaction. He feels small and frail.
Zh: 塑料斗篷感觉很粗糙。男人一阵轻微地颤抖——除此之外，没有别的反应。他感觉自己渺小又脆弱。

En: He's going into some kind of... psycho-motor immobility. The lieutenant inspects him gently. "The good news is -- this solves our transportation problem. Doesn't it Mr. Dros?"
Zh: “他进入了某种……意识活动的固定状态。”警督小心地检查着他。“好消息是——这就解决了我们的运输问题。不是吗，德罗斯先生？”

En: Between this and the broken tyre he's used for a boat -- I think it's safe to leave him here, while we go and get help. It will need to be medical first, I'm afraid.
Zh: “介于此，还有他用作小船的破轮胎——我觉得把他留在这里很安全，我们可以去寻求帮助。不过我恐怕，首先需要医疗援助。”

En: The trembling mouth appears to sigh.
Zh: 颤抖的嘴唇似乎在叹息。

En: Mr. Dros?
Zh: “德罗斯先生？”

En: The trembling mouth appears to sigh. That's all. You've fried him.
Zh: 颤抖的嘴唇似乎在叹息。只有这些。你瓦解了他的防线。

En: What happened to you, Mr. Dros?
Zh: “你怎么了，德罗斯先生？”

En: What has happened to this man?
Zh: “这人怎么了？”

En: I think he's addicted to that thing.
Zh: “我觉得他对那个东西上瘾了。”

En: Could it be there's something hormonal in his relationship to the phasmid.
Zh: “他跟竹节虫之间的关系会不会有些荷尔蒙的成分？”

En: Hang tight. [Leave.]
Zh: “等我一下。”[离开。]

En: Old age and shock. He looks at him, then you.
Zh: “年纪大了，而且受惊过度。”他看着他，然后是你。

En: I think it's the *phasmid*.
Zh: “我觉得是因为*竹节虫*。”

En: Old age and shock, yes.
Zh: “年纪大，再加上受到惊吓，没错。”

En: The appearance of the phasmid, in conjunction with the stress of the arrest. He's spent his entire life here. For him to leave would be... He shakes his head.
Zh: “竹节虫的出现，再加上被逮捕的压力。他在这里待了一辈子。离开这里，对于他来说……”他摇摇头。

En: Yes. The arrest and the appearance of the phasmid -- the combined stress... He looks at you. "But you think it's something more than that, don't you?"
Zh: “是的。被逮捕，还有竹节虫的出现——两者的压力加在一起……”他看着你。“不过你觉得还不仅仅是这样，对吗？”

En: He couldn't *see* it, Kim. It's just the reeds for him.
Zh: “他*看不见*它，金。对于他来说，那只是芦苇。”

En: He couldn't *see* it, Cuno. It's just the reeds for him.
Zh: “他*看不见*它，坤诺。对于他来说，那只是芦苇。”

En: He couldn't *see* it, Cuno. It's just the reeds for him.
Zh: “他*看不见*它，坤诺。对于他来说，那只是芦苇。”

En: He can't *see* it, Kim. It's just the reeds for him.
Zh: “他*看不见*它，金。对于他来说，那只是芦苇。”

En: That could be part of the shock. But you're right -- something is off here. Mr. Dros... He touches the man's shoulder.
Zh: “那可能也是他感到震惊的一部分。不过你说得对——好像有点不太对劲。德罗斯先生……”他拍拍男人的肩膀。

En: No response.
Zh: 没有回应。

En: Before -- when I evaluated his state -- he seemed strangely animated.
Zh: “在这之前——在我评估他的状态的时候——他似乎出奇地有活力。”

En: I *talked* to the phasmid. It said it's destroying him.
Zh: “我跟竹节虫*聊过*了。它说它在摧毁他。”

En: Maybe. Anyway -- it's only trying to remain unseen. The degradation is a side effect.
Zh: “也许吧。总之——它只是试着隐藏自己。退化只是一种副作用。”

En: Maybe this is how the phasmid has stayed hidden all these years?
Zh: “也许这么多年以来，竹节虫就是这样隐匿自己的吧？”

En: Then how did we see it? He thinks. "Oh, you mean whatever does this, does it over *time*? Teenagers, kids, drunks -- sightings are brief. And hence not credible. But anyone who spends a long time with it..."
Zh: “那为什么我们可以看见它？”他想到。“哦，你的意思是不管是什么原因造成的，都是随着*时间*造成的？青少年，小孩，醉鬼——目击是短暂的。因此并不可靠。任何和它长时间待在一起的人……”

En: Then how did *we* see it? He thinks. "Oh, you mean whatever does this, does it over *time*? Teenagers, kids, drunks -- sightings are brief. And hence not credible. Anyone who spends a long time with it..."
Zh: “那为什么*我们*可以看见？”他想到。“哦，你的意思是不管是什么原因造成的，都是随着*时间*造成的？青少年，小孩，醉鬼——目击是短暂的。因此并不可靠。任何和它长时间待在一起的人……”

En: Yes. You *forget* it's there.
Zh: “是的。你*忘记*它在那里了。”

En: Mhm. The lieutenant inspects the man. "Mr. Dros -- have you *ever* seen a stick insect, pretending to be the reeds?"
Zh: “嗯。”警督观察着老人。“德罗斯先生——你*曾经*有没有见过一只竹节虫，假装成芦苇的样子？”

En: Th-the... The old man stutters.
Zh: “那-那个……”老人结结巴巴。

En: The doctors will have to look at this. I hope your station has better medical personnel than 57. This is... a little advanced for a nurse.
Zh: “需要找医生来检查一下。希望你们分局的医疗人员比57分局的好一些。对于护士来说，这个状况有点……太超前了。”

En: He nods. "He was energetic and articulate. After all these years alone, with little hygiene or medication I would expect worse."
Zh: 他点点头。“他的确精力充沛，而且口齿清晰。这么多年的独处，没有什么卫生保健或者是药物治疗，我本来以为情况会更糟糕一些。”

En: Perhaps this *animation* is induced by something in the phasmid?
Zh: “也许这种*犀利*是那只竹节虫身上的什么东西引起的？”

En: Hmm... He looks at the creature...
Zh: “嗯……”他看着那个生物……

En: He does not seem to be *animated* now it's left... He looks to the sea. "Honestly, I'm ready to believe anything at this point. Maybe it *is* psychoactive."
Zh: “现在它离开了，他似乎就没那么*有活力*了……”他望向大海。“老实说，此时此刻，我已经做好了相信任何东西的准备。也许它的确*能*影响别人的心理状态。”

En: Spinning in silence -- limbs and antennae, like hair.
Zh: 沉默地旋转着——节肢和触须，像头发丝一样。

En: It's *three metres* tall. An undiscovered species. I'm not ready to believe in *anything* -- but yes, I'm ready to believe it has psychoactive qualities.
Zh: “它有*三米*高。是一种未被发现的物种。我还没准备好相信*任何事*——不过，没错，我可以相信它拥有影响他人心理状态的能力。”

En: Which is why we should be very, very wary of approaching it again.
Zh: “这也就是我们再次接近它的时候，一定要非常、非常小心的原因。”

En: Like a drug?
Zh: “像一种毒品吗？”

En: He has the symptoms of an addict. And not the *normal* symptoms.
Zh: “他表现出了上瘾的症状。而且不是*普通*的症状。”

En: He *has* displayed addict behaviour. And not *just* to painkillers.
Zh: “他*已经*表现出了上瘾行为。而且*不仅仅*是对止痛药。”

En: It's... as if he's infatuated with it.
Zh: “就好像……他对它着迷了。”

En: His pupils appeared to be dilated. They still are... He examines the catatonic man's eyes.
Zh: “他的瞳孔好像扩张了。现在还是……”他检查着紧张症患者的眼睛。

En: It's not just chemical.
Zh: 这不仅仅是化学。

En: No, it's not that.
Zh: 不，不是这样的。

En: Some kind of oxytocin release mechanism? Would go with the pupils. But this is way above me, detective.
Zh: “某种催产素释放机制？对瞳孔产生了影响。这个已经远远超出我的理解范围了，警探。”

En: The man shudders lightly, his eyes swallowed up by their pupils.
Zh: 男人轻轻颤抖着，他的眼睛被瞳孔吞没了。

En: You mean pheromonal?
Zh: “你的意思是信息素？”

En: He seemed... a little *off* for a man his age. Randy.
Zh: “对于他这种年纪的男人来说，他似乎……有点*失常*。冲动过头。”

En: Humans are humans. Do you need... neurological tampering for the *glands* to work? I don't know...
Zh: “人类就是人类。是不是需要……神经性干预才能让*腺体*工作，我不知道……”

En: The scope, knowing of her bruises, his *disposition* toward Miss Oranje... He nods. "I see what you mean."
Zh: “瞄准镜，知道她身上的瘀伤，他对奥兰治小姐的*性情*……”他点点头。“我明白你的意思。”

En: He's been here for a long time. Who knows how much of it in its company? (Conclude.)
Zh: “他在这里待了很长时间。谁知道它陪伴了他多久？”（总结。）

En: You should be more careful, detective. Are you sure it wasn't having an *effect* on you?
Zh: “你应该对未知物种更小心一些，警探。你确定它不会对你造成什么*影响*吗？”

En: No way. I was only talking to it.
Zh: “不可能的。我只是跟它说说话而已。”

En: A valid hunch. Long term exposure to something like that could be neurodegenerative... Also, please be careful when approaching unknown species in the future, detective.
Zh: “这种直觉是有根据的。长时间暴露在某种可能是神经变性的东西面前……还有，以后在接近未知物种的时候，请务必小心一些，警探。”

En: Are you sure? You were looking at it for a *long* time. Almost as if you were hypnotized. He thinks. "Were you hypnotized?"
Zh: “你确定吗？你看了它*很长*一段时间。好像你都被催眠了一样。”他想到。“你被催眠了吗？”

En: It's definitely toxic -- the phasmid. It told me it is.
Zh: “它肯定有毒——那只竹节虫。它告诉我的。”

En: Told you? Yes. Good. During your *long* staring match. I understand. He looks at the man, then you.
Zh: “告诉你？是啊，很好。在你那*漫长*的瞪眼比赛中。我能理解。”他先是看着男人，然后看着你。

En: He did seem distressed when it finally came to arresting him. Like he didn't want to leave this place. And the insect maybe... He looks at his notebook.
Zh: “最后要逮捕他的时候，他的确好像很忧虑。就像他不想离开这个地方。还有那个昆虫也许……”他看着自己的笔记本。

En: I have absolutely forgotten to take notes. I hope I remember all of this. He shakes his head in disbelief. "This will be one hell of a report -- thank *god* we have the photo."
Zh: “我完全忘记要做笔记了。希望我还能全想起来。”他怀疑地摇摇头。“这份报告真是够要命的——*谢天谢地*，我们还有相片。”

En: I have absolutely forgotten to take notes -- I hope I remember all of this. He shakes his head. "This will be one hell of a report -- they'll think we're insane."
Zh: “我完全忘记笔记的事了——希望我还能全想起来。“他摇摇头。“这份报告绝对够要命的——他们肯定会以为我们疯了。”

En: Quite a few things about that health check you did on him make sense now.
Zh: 在给他做健康检查时发现的一些东西，现在也能说通了。

En: There's *much* more. Remember what it said, when it spoke.
Zh: 还*不止*如此。还记得它是怎么说的吗。

En: He stares into the reeds. Your words don't stir anything in him. Perhaps you should...
Zh: 他凝视着芦苇丛。你的话语没有对他产生任何影响。也许你应该……

En: Tap on the helmet on your head.
Zh: 拍拍你头上的头盔。

En: Show him the ceramic helmet.
Zh: 把陶瓷头盔给他看。

En: Show him the detached scope.
Zh: 把拆下来的瞄准镜给他看。

En: Show him the Oranjese passport.
Zh: 把奥兰治护照拿给他看。

En: I'm going to let you rest now Mr. Dros. (Conclude.)
Zh: “现在我先让你休息一下，德罗斯先生。”（总结。）

En: We should think about getting back to the mainland. To get help. He'll be safe here. If we don't take too long...
Zh: “我们应该考虑先回内陆。去寻求帮助。他在这里很安全。如果我们抓紧时间的话……”

En: I... He turns his eyes to it. "I lost..."
Zh: “我……”他看着它。“我弄丢……”

En: You lost it, Mr. Dros?
Zh: “你把它弄丢了，对吗，德罗斯先生？”

En: He turns his eyes to the reeds again -- as he's done so many times. Beige and white stripes...
Zh: 他再次把目光转回到芦苇丛——就像他经常做的一样。米黄色，白条纹……

En: He lost the scope. Then it somehow made its way over there. With the help of a magpie phasmid? The lieutenant observes the lens sparkle in your hand.
Zh: “他把瞄准镜弄丢了。然后不知怎么的，它又跑到那边去了。是那个有收集癖的竹节虫帮的忙？”警督端详着你手中闪闪发亮的镜片。

En: This sight is a T-9, Mr. Dros. Was it attached to the rifle, when you made the shot?
Zh: “这是T-9瞄准镜，德罗斯先生。你开枪的时候，它是不是装在步枪上的？”

En: Silence. Not even a sigh.
Zh: 沉默。甚至连叹息都没有。

En: You've gotten all you will out of this poor being.
Zh: 你已经从这个可怜人那里得到了想要的一切。

En: I mean, why not? It's three metres tall... He takes off his glasses and cleans them -- when he puts them back on he's still staring at the sea.
Zh: “我的意思是，为什么不呢？它有三米高……”他取下眼镜擦拭着——再次戴回去的时候，他还在凝望大海。

En: Nothing. Just dull staring -- not even rage left wherever he is.
Zh: 没反应。只是呆呆地看着——甚至连愤怒都没有留下。

En: The last embers have gone out. The war is over.
Zh: 最后的余烬已经熄灭。战争结束了。

En: If Cuno kicked it into the sea, as he said he did -- the ebb would pull it back here. This makes sense. Mr. Dros could have picked it up. Or... the phasmid, even? If it did, this is incredible...
Zh: “如果坤诺把它踢进海里去了，就像他说的——那退潮会把它送回这里。这也能说得通。德罗斯先生可能把它捡了起来。或者……甚至是竹节虫做的？如果是它，这就太不可思议了……”

En: No reaction. His breathing is slow and he appears very old all of a sudden. Around eighty.
Zh: 没有反应。他的呼吸很平缓，霎那间他显得异常苍老。80岁左右。

En: This is an old man -- at last. No longer a tin soldier, but the broken down remains of a man.
Zh: 这是一个老人——终归如此。不再是一个坏掉的锡兵，而是一个男人的残躯。

En: Let's have a closer look at that, after. I think I'll be more useful than him... He nods toward the old man.
Zh: “之后我们再仔细调查一下吧。我想我应该比他有用一些……”他朝着老人点点头。

En: The plastic cape flaps around his face, in a gust of wind. His back is slouched and his mouth open.
Zh: 一阵风吹来，塑料斗篷在他的脸庞周围扑腾。他佝偻着后背，嘴巴大张着。

En: The blacks of his eyes are receding, his pupils are returning to normal.
Zh: 眼睛里的黑色逐渐褪去，瞳孔正在恢复正常。

En: The strength has all gone out of him -- just frail old bones in a sack of tracksuit trousers and a wind breaker.
Zh: 他全身的气力已经耗尽——只剩下一副脆弱的老骨头，包裹在运动服裤子和防风夹克衫里。

En: Did you take this passport and other papers from a buoy on the coast?
Zh: “这个护照，还有海岸边浮标里的其他文件，都是你拿走的吗？”

En: He blinks and continues to stare at the reeds. "The... spirit."
Zh: 他眨眨眼，继续盯着芦苇丛。“那个……幽灵。”

En: He hears us... The spirit?
Zh: “他听见我们的话了……幽灵？”

En: No reply. He's gone again.
Zh: 没有回应。他又走神了。

En: ... is what you plan to say. Before you can get past 'you're under', the kid interrupts you:
Zh: ……你计划这么说。在你还没来得及说出‘你被……’之前，男孩就打断了你：

En: You sure? He whispers. "You sure we got it all?"
Zh: “你确定吗？”他小声说到。“你确定我们都查清楚了？”

En: Hey... The kid interrupts you at 'you're under', his voice subdued. "You sure we got all this shit?"
Zh: “喂……”男子在你说出‘你被逮’的时候打断了你，他压低了声音：“你确定我们查清楚所有破事了？”

En: (Nod.) "We've been thorough."
Zh: （点点头。）“我们已经问的很彻底了。”

En: (Turn to the suspect.) "One more thing, Mr. Dros..."
Zh: （转向嫌疑人。）“还有一件事，德罗斯先生……”

En: Right. He turns to him. "Give him his rights."
Zh: “好吧。”男孩转向他。“给他权利！”

En: What?! The old man's eyes fill with sudden, unexpected terror at the words: "But you said I would be taken to the, uhm, the..."
Zh: “什么？！”因为这些话语，老人的眼睛里突然闪现出意料之外的恐惧。“但你不是说我会被送到……呃，那个……”

En: The wind picks up. The silence on the water is broken all around you, little shivers of waves appear...
Zh: 起风了。周围水面的寂静被打破了，海浪微微颤抖……

En: This terror -- the sum of all the uncontrollable movements and mood swings he's been exhibiting...
Zh: 这种恐惧——是所有无法控制的动作，还有他表现出来的情绪波动的总和……

En: The sum of all the erratic movements, fidgeting and mood swings he's been exhibiting...
Zh: 所有古怪动作，烦躁，还有他表现出来的情绪波动的总和……

En: I... He looks around, confused.
Zh: “我……”他环顾四周，样子很困惑。

En: Do you understand, sir?
Zh: “你明白了吗，先生？”

En: No... I don't want to. I have to stay *here*. He looks at the reeds, eyes full of terror.
Zh: “不……我不想。我必须待在*这里*。”他看着芦苇丛，眼里的恐惧越积越多。

En: He's sweating. Beads are forming on his forehead.
Zh: 他在流汗，额头上出现了豆大的汗珠。

En: He is a brave man -- why is he unravelling?
Zh: 他是个勇敢的男人——为什么会崩溃呢？

En: He's not afraid of jail. He's afraid of something else...
Zh: 他不害怕监狱。他在害怕别的……

En: Pupils are dilated too, eyes getting blacker and blacker...
Zh: 瞳孔也在扩张，眼珠变得越来越黑……

En: Like parting from a loved one.
Zh: 就像跟爱人分离。

En: We don't need your permisson, the kid turns to you. "We do need to talk about that *boat* though. How are we gonna take him?"
Zh: “我们不需要你的许可，”男孩转向你。“不过，我们要聊聊*船*的事。我们怎么带走他？”

En: Do you understand?
Zh: “你明白了吗？”

En: Wait...
Zh: 等等……

En: Inland Empire warned.
Zh: 本心在警告。

En: Jamais vu...
Zh: 旧事如新……

En: Col Do Ma Ma Daqua
Zh: 哈库多玛达塔

En: Shush, Cuno! Do you hear that?
Zh: “嘘，坤诺！你听见了吗？”

En: There it is again, to your north, as it has been since you came to the coast -- the reeds whisper, stalks rubbing against each other. But then, in the middle of it...
Zh: 又来了，在你北边，自从你来到这个海岸，它就一直在那里——芦苇在低语，茎杆互相摩擦。不过，在那中间……

En: ...and then it's gone. Drowned out by the kid's voice -- "Maybe there *is* room for three on the boat..."
Zh: ……然后就没了。淹没在男孩的声音里——“也许船上*有*位置够我们三个人……”

En: ...and then it's gone. Drowned out by the kid's voice -- "Maybe there is room for three on the boat..."
Zh: ……然后就没了。淹没在男孩的声音里——“也许船上有位置够我们三个人……”

En: You thought you heard something but it's just the reeds.
Zh: 你觉得自己听见了某种声音，不过那里只有芦苇……

En: What?
Zh: 什么？

En: Something completely different. It sounds like a bow, very slowly being drawn against the strings of a violin. A very small violin made of reeds and rushes.
Zh: 某种完全不同的东西。听起来像一支琴弓，非常缓慢地在小提琴的弦上拉动着。一个用芦苇和蒲草做成的小提琴。

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: You're saying we can all fit in there?
Zh: “你是说我们都能塞进去？”

En: Maybe we could all fit in there...
Zh: “也许我们都能塞进去……”

En: Your skin crawls.
Zh: 你皮肤上的汗毛竖了起来。

En: Why not?
Zh: “为什么不呢？”

En: What a farce! What are you *talking* about? Is this... The old man's voice drowns in a sudden gust of wind. "... really us..."
Zh: “真是一场闹剧！你们*说*什么呢？这是……”老人的声音突然被一阵狂风吞没了。“……真的，我们……”

En: Thank god. If he can see, then you're not insane.
Zh: 谢天谢地。如果他可以看见，那你就没有发疯。

En: I can see it.
Zh: “我能看见。”

En: You feel the kid tug on your sleeve -- and in the darkness -- four words:
Zh: 你感觉男孩拽着你的袖子——在黑暗中——只说了四个字：

En: Be *very* careful.... the kid whispers, then takes a step toward the giant arthropod...
Zh: “但是要*非常*小心……”男孩低语到，然后朝着巨大的节肢动物靠近了一步……

En: Thank god. If *he* can see, then *maybe* you're not insane.
Zh: 谢天谢地。如果*他*可以看见，那你就没有发疯。

En: Yeah, I can fuckin' see it!
Zh: “哦耶，我他妈的能看见它！”

En: But that would mean...
Zh: 但是那就意味着……

En: But that means...
Zh: 但那意味着……

En: It's really there. Spinning slowly -- in absolute silence -- its limbs long and slender...
Zh: 它真的在那里。慢慢地旋转——在绝对的沉默中——它的节肢修长而纤细……

En: It's really there. Spinning slowly -- in absolute silence -- its limbs long and slender...
Zh: 它真的在那里。慢慢地旋转——在绝对的沉默中——它的节肢修长而纤细……

En: If he can see it too, then you're not *totally* insane.
Zh: 如果他也看见了，那你*完全*没有发疯。

En: Yeah, old man. Hang tight. He looks at you. "Let's *slowly* start shufflin' off Death Island, okay? We're about done here..." He looks to the flak tower.
Zh: “是啊，老头。等一下。”他看着你。“我们*慢慢地*离开死亡岛，好吗？这里已经差不多完事了……”他看向高射炮塔。

En: The old man holds himself for warmth. His gaze is vacantly directed toward the mainland.
Zh: 老人抱紧自己，寻求温暖。眼睛依旧茫然地看向内陆。

En: He's old and fried. Cuno's seen this... like, after a massive bender. Cuno's dad... He stops mid sentence.
Zh: “他老了，受惊过度。坤诺见过这个……就像，疯狂灌了一大堆酒之后的坤诺的老爹一样……”他话说到一半就停了下来。

En: Yeah and then there's that shit... He looks at him with pity. "Gramps couldn't take it. It was too much."
Zh: “是啊，还有那个狗屎……”他怜悯地看着他。“老头子受不了。太过头了。”

En: I mean, getting arrested as a fucking GIANT stick insect jumps up behind you... He shakes his head.  "That shit would break *any* man. Even a Cuno Man."
Zh: “我的意思是，就像有一只他妈的巨大的竹节虫从你背后跳了出来，说要逮捕你……”他摇摇头。“那这种狗屁事情，*任何人*都受不了的。就连坤诺也是。”

En: Yeah-yeah, you see that too? He looks at the unresponsive man. "Got a little jumpy there didn't you? I know that vibe. I'm off my lightning too..."
Zh: “是啊，你也看见了？”他看着毫无反应的老人。“有点神经质了，是吗？我知道那种感觉。我也没嗑闪电丸了……”

En: Told you? When you were doing your *long* staring thing? He looks at the man, then you. "You're one crazy cop, you know? Cuno digs it."
Zh: “告诉你？在你*久久地*盯着它看的时候？”他看着老人，然后看着你。“你是个疯子警察，你知道吗？坤诺喜欢。”

En: Talked, huh? You sure it didn't have a strange effect on, *you*? You looked, like, hypnotized.
Zh: “说过话，哈？你确定它没有对*你*有什么奇怪的影响吗？你看起来，好像，被催眠了。”

En: No way. I was only talking to it.
Zh: “不可能的。我只是跟它说说话而已。”

En: Maybe. Anyway -- it's only trying to remain unseen. The degradation is a side effect.
Zh: “也许吧。总之——它只是试着隐藏自己。退化只是一种副作用。”

En: Yeah? Just talking. Sure. You were *deep* in some trance shit.
Zh: “是吗？只是说说话。当然了。你他妈都*陷入*他妈的恍惚状态了好吗。”

En: Yeah, man -- I don't know. I don't know what *degradation* means. And I don't know about getting close to it like that -- and staring at it for five minutes... You're one crazy cop.
Zh: “哟，老兄——我不知道。我不知道*退化*是什么意思。我也不知道要不要离得它那么近——盯着它整整5分钟……你真是个疯子警察。”

En: You mean like *horny*?
Zh: “你的意思是*性冲动*？”

En: Yes. He seemed... a little *off* for a man his age.
Zh: “是的。对于他这个年纪的人来说……他似乎有点*不对劲*。”

En: I mean, he was perving on that Klaasje party lady weirdly hard... He nods. "Yeah... maybe?"
Zh: “我的意思是，他特别迷恋那个叫卡拉洁的派对女郎……”他点点头。“是啊……也许吧？”

En: I don't see how bugs help with that shit... Like? How? Anyway...
Zh: “我没看出这只昆虫是怎么帮上忙的……比如说？怎么做到的？无所谓了……”

En: Like, hooked on the lightning?
Zh: “就像，闪电丸上瘾？”

En: True... Cuno leans into the man's face, stares into his eyes. "He's got those *gurn eyes* doesn't he?"
Zh: “没错……”坤诺靠近老人的脸，直勾勾地盯着他的眼睛。“他的眼睛挺*古怪*的，不是吗？”

En: Whoah, like love? The kid stares at you. Looks like he's trying to put together a puzzle -- one which he gives up on immediately. "Yeah, no, I don't know..."
Zh: “哇哦，就像恋爱吗？”男孩盯着你。看起来他在试着解决一个难题——不过他瞬间就放弃了。“哦耶，不，我不知道……”

En: Uh... He looks at the creature.
Zh: “嗯……”他看着那个生物。

En: Yeah-yeah. Because -- look at him now. Not so *sharp* is he? And it's gone, left. Perhaps it's, like, kept him going like the drink and the lightning keep my old man...
Zh: “是啊，是啊。因为——现在看看他。没有那么*犀利*了是不是？而且它走了，离开了。也许它，比如说，能让他坚持下去，就像我老爹靠酒精和闪电丸续命一样……”

En: Fuck it, why not? A big ghost insect like that is probably going to fuck all your shit.
Zh: “去他妈的，为什么不呢？那种像幽灵一样的大昆虫很可能会把你搞得乱七八糟的。”

En: Spinning in silence -- limbs and antennae, like hair.
Zh: 沉默地旋转着——节肢和触须，像头发丝一样。

En: Yeah, the kid says, interested. "Cuno knows hobos. And Cuno has never seen a hobo sharp like this, after so many years of hoboing."
Zh: “哦耶，”男孩兴致勃勃地说到。“坤诺知道流浪汉。坤诺从没见过像他这样犀利的流浪汉，更别说他已经流浪这么多年了。”

En: Maybe this is how the phasmid has stayed hidden all these years?
Zh: “也许这么多年以来，竹节虫就是这样隐匿自己的吧？”

En: Then how the *fuck* did we see it? He scratches his head. "Or -- wait! The longer it's there. Like it needs time to sink in and if you spend time with it..."
Zh: “那我们*他妈的*为什么能看见？”他挠挠头。“或者——等一下！时间比较长。比如说，需要时间渗透，而且，如果你跟它待在一起的话……”

En: Then how the *fuck* do we see it? He scratches his head. "Or -- wait! The longer it's there. Like it needs time to sink in and if you spend time with it..."
Zh: “那我们*他妈的*为什么能看见？”他挠挠头。“或者——等一下！时间比较长。比如说，需要时间渗透，而且，如果你跟它待在一起的话……”

En: You *forget* it's there.
Zh: “你就*忘记*它在那里了。”

En: His eyes widen, as if to help him take the thought in. He prods the old man. "Yo -- you ever *seen* a giant fucking insect?"
Zh: 他睁大眼睛，似乎这样能帮他接受这种想法。他戳了戳老人。“哟——你有没有*见过*一只大昆虫？”

En: Th-the... The old man stutters. Then a sigh.
Zh: “那-那个……”老人结结巴巴。然后又是一声叹息。

En: How? Like -- it's pretty fucking huge... He nudges the man, gently: "How?"
Zh: “怎么回事？比如说——它他妈的特别大……”他轻轻地推了推老人：“怎么回事？”

En: I can't *believe* that helmet, man. I kicked it into the fucking ocean and it washed up here. Or... do you -- do you think the *phasmid* brought it here? He squints, looking around.
Zh: “我不敢*相信*，那个头盔，老兄，我他妈的把它踢到海里去了，结果它又跑这里来了。或者……你——你会不会觉得是*竹节虫*把它带过来的？”他眯起眼睛，环顾四周。

En: About that... Cuno squints at the helmet. "Me and C kicked that shit into the ocean. She took it off the corpse and kicked it, rugby style. How'd it wash up here..." He pauses -- then squints. "I don't know."
Zh: “关于那个……”坤诺眯起眼睛，看着那个偷窥。“我和坤妮把那破玩意提到海里去了。她从尸体上拿下来，然后踹了一脚，像玩橄榄球一样。它是怎么会冲到这里来的……”他停顿下来——眯起眼睛。“我不知道。”

En: Hey -- hey. He waves his hand under his nose. "You took the shot -- bang! -- and got rid of the evidence. *Bug* brought it back. Sound about right?"
Zh: “喂——喂。”他在老人的鼻子下面挥了挥手。“你开的枪——嘣！——然后丢掉了证物。*昆虫*又把它捡回来了。是这么回事吗？”

En: He has the symptoms of an addict. And not the normal symptoms.
Zh: “他表现出了上瘾的症状。而且不是*普通*的症状。”

En: He *has* displayed addict behaviour. And not just to painkillers.
Zh: “他*已经*表现出了上瘾行为。而且*不仅仅*是止痛药。”

En: The boy waves his hand in front of the old man's face.
Zh: 男孩在老人面前挥挥手。

En: The trembling mouth appears to sigh. There's no other response.
Zh: 颤抖的嘴唇像是在叹息。没有其他回应。

En: Yeah, this fried him. We gotta bring him to a doctor. Good news -- this solves our boat-and-piggies problem. He's not going anywhere. He's trapped here...
Zh: “是啊，他崩溃了。我们要带他去看医生。好消息是——这就解决了船和猪头过河的问题。他哪里都不会去。他被困在这里了……”

En: What was that about *steroids*? It piqued your interest...
Zh: *类固醇*是怎么回事？它激起了你的兴趣……

En: There was something about a statue, and nihilistic advertising agency people? Might be worth investigating...
Zh: 那一尊雕像好像有什么问题，还有那些虚无主义的广告公司的人？可能值得调查……

En: And the soucriant, on *her* yacht -- Joyce? Probably...
Zh: 还有邪恶女巫，乘着*她的*游艇——乔伊斯吗？有可能……

En: Boom-boom morons on thin ice, going under -- those are the tent kids!
Zh: 薄冰上那些吵吵闹闹的低能儿，沉没——是那些帐篷里的孩子！

En: Yo, Cuno doesn't understand what's goin' on here. Get a hold of it, detective Dick, let's move on, okay?
Zh: “哟，坤诺不明白这是怎么一回事。稳住，迪克警探，我们继续，好吗？”

En: I can't *believe* the inert lumpen out there. They just let her drive her little boat like that. No violence, not even a robbery. The working class has lost their appetite for justice... His voice trails off in disappointment.
Zh: “我不*相信*那些痴痴呆呆的流浪汉居然会这样。让她开着那只小船到处游荡。没有暴力行动，甚至都没有抢劫。工人阶级已经失去了伸张正义的欲望……”他的声音因为失望而变得消沉。

En: If only he had the ammunition.
Zh: 如果他有弹药就好了。

En: Sometimes Cuno likes to look at the baggie before he does it. Just look at that fat baggie...
Zh: “有时候坤诺喜欢看着那个垃圾袋。看着那个大胖垃圾袋……”

En: Momentarily he's worried for him. He reminds him of himself. The same hatred. The same... you try to think of another thing -- but no, it's just the hatred.
Zh: 他暂时为他担心了一阵。他让他想到了自己。相同的仇恨。一模一样……你试着想出别的什么东西——不过没有，只有仇恨。

En: They are veterans of the same war. Somewhere west, in Coal City his enemy is lying in a dark room, on a metal slider, not breathing. Like he will too. Soon. Half of the war between these men is over.
Zh: 他们是经历过同一场战争的老兵。在西边的某个地方，煤城里，他的敌人躺在一个黑暗的房间里，一个金属滑块上，没有了呼吸。他也会这样。很快。这些人之间的战争已经结束了一半。

En: With an agitated gait Satellite-Officer Jean-Heron Vicquemare paces the jetty, 22 kilometres East -- in Martinaise: "What could he *possibly* be doing there for so long," he says.
Zh: 随迁警官让·‘苍鹭’·维克玛激动地走向码头，东边22公里处——在马丁内斯：“他怎么*可能*在那里待那么久，”他说到。

En: Policework? Patrol Officer Judit Minot replies, with a tinge of hope in her voice. A tinge of warmth too, against the cold air rising from the water...
Zh: “警察工作？”巡警茱蒂特·迈诺特回复到，声音里夹杂着些许希望。还有一丝温暖，对抗着水面吹来的冷风……

En: He's drunk, she thinks. And we all know it. Still, she's just *trying* to see it in a  generous light...
Zh: 他喝醉了，她想到。我们都知道这一点。不过，她还是*尝试*以一种宽容的角度来看待这个问题……

En: He's drunk, she thinks. And we all know it. Still, she's just *trying* to see it in a  generous light...
Zh: 他喝醉了，她想到。我们都知道这一点。不过，她还是*尝试*以一种宽容的角度来看待这个问题……

En: With an agitated gait Satellite-Officer Jean-Heron Vicquemare paces the jetty, 22 kilometres East -- in Martinaise: "What could he *possibly* be doing there for so long," he says.
Zh: 随迁警官让·‘苍鹭’·维克玛激动地走向码头，东边22公里处——在马丁内斯：“他怎么*可能*在那里待那么久，”他说到。

En: Policework? Patrol Officer Judit Minot replies, with a tinge of hope in her voice. A tinge of warmth too, against the cold air rising from the water...
Zh: “警察工作？”巡警茱蒂特·迈诺特回复到，声音里夹杂着些许希望。还有一丝温暖，对抗着水面吹来的冷风……

En: I'm sorry, Mr. Dros, the lieutenant says, softly. "I understand you knew him for a long time."
Zh: “很抱歉，德罗斯先生。”警督柔声说到。“我明白，你认识他很久了。”

En: They're all dead now. He just shakes his head. "Fuck it..."
Zh: “现在他们都死了。”他只是摇摇头。“该死……”

En: If he really wanted to kill him so bad, he would have done. There must have been a thousand *black days* on these islands, his health ailing.
Zh: 如果他真的那么想杀他，早就会这样做了。这座岛上肯定有几千个*黑暗的日子*，他的身体健康每况愈下。

En: I'm sorry.
Zh: “我很抱歉。”

En: You had a thousand chances to kill him.
Zh: “你有1000次杀死他的机会。”

En: And I blew them all. What does it matter now? He's gone. Ancient dust.
Zh: “而我把它们全都浪费掉了。现在又有什么关系呢？他走了。化为尘土。”

En: All human beings care about each other. A little flash of anger. "I cared for... seeing his head explode. And now... god damn this world."
Zh: 一丝愤怒掠过。“所有人都会在乎彼此。我关心的是……看见别人的脑袋开花。结果现在……这个世界真可恨。”

En: You cared about him?
Zh: “你在乎他？”

En: He reminded him of himself. The same hatred. The same... You try to think of something else, but no, it's just hatred.
Zh: 他让他想到了自己。相同的仇恨。一模一样……你试着想出别的什么东西，不过没有，只有仇恨。

En: Fuck you... the old communard says -- staring at the ground -- seemingly to the island you're on.
Zh: “去你妈的……”老公社社员说到——眼睛盯着地面——似乎是对你脚下这座小岛说的。

En: That's it! That's what you weren't connecting yourself...
Zh: 就是它！这就是你没有把自己联系在一起的地方……

En: Ask about that 'cock on parade' too. Make sure you get everything here.
Zh: 顺便问问那个‘游行的大公鸡’。保证你把一切都搞明白。

En: His lip curls into a sneer at the memory.
Zh: 他撅起嘴唇，嘲笑着回忆。

En: Animated? By what?
Zh: 被激活了？被什么激活了？

En: Perhaps. But his seems more than that. The inner turmoil takes unexpected turns, as if forced on him in a way...
Zh: 也许。不过似乎远不止那样。内心的骚动产生意料之外的剧变，在某种程度上，仿佛在强迫他一样……

En: Is it some kind of substance damage? Like he's addicted to something -- not only the painkillers he's clearly on.
Zh: 是某种物质上的伤害吗？比如说他对什么东西上瘾了——显然不仅仅是止痛药。

En: More specifically?
Zh: “再具体一点？”

En: A tragicomedy... He shakes to life. "Druggies, prostitutes and rentiers."
Zh: “一出悲喜剧……”他颤抖着活了过来。“瘾君子，妓女还有吃息族。”

En: For a sixty-year-old man with stomach trouble -- who's spent his entire life alone on an uninhabited island -- he seems surprisingly fit.
Zh: 对于一个有胃疾的60岁老人来说——终其一生都孤独地生活在一个无人岛——他似乎出奇地健康。

En: That's it? (Squint at him.)
Zh: 就这样？（眯起眼睛看他。）

En: He's clearly a drug user of the painkillers sort, prone to erratic hand gestures -- and malnourished. But that's it. The moment passes and you say...
Zh: 他明显对某种止痛剂上瘾，有做古怪手势的倾向——还有营养不良。不过就这样吧。过了一会儿，你开口说到……

En: Aside from the rare erratic hand gesture, smacking his lips and despondently staring into the fire, you can only see that he is old.
Zh: 除了非常古怪的手势，砸吧嘴唇和沮丧地凝视着火堆之外，你只能看出他老了。

En: He's prone to erratic hand gestures -- and clearly malnourished. But that's it. You can see no more by looking at his slouched frame. The moment passes and you say...
Zh: 他有做古怪手势的倾向——而且明显是营养不良。不过就这样了。看着他没精打采的样子，你也看不出什么了。过了一会儿，你开口说到……

En: What strikes you about this gaunt man is not the stomach pain, or the cough, or the malnutrition. It's precisely what you could *not* see before. For a man who's spent 44 years hidden in the urban wild...
Zh: 你注意到这个枯瘦男人的原因不是胃痛，或者咳嗽，或者营养不良。而是你之前正好*没有*看见的东西。对于一个在城市荒野中躲藏了44年的人来说……

En: What strikes you about this gaunt man is not the stomach pain, or the cough, or the malnutrition. For a man who's spent 44 years hidden in the urban wild...
Zh: 你注意到这个枯瘦男人的原因不是胃痛，或者咳嗽，或者营养不良。而是你之前正好*没有*看见的东西。对于一个在城市荒野中躲藏了44年的人来说……

En: Indeed. He speaks fluidly, his movements are rapid, if erratic. His voice, despite the cough, is there. It is capable of expressing complicated ideas. Above all, he seems *animated*.
Zh: 的确。他言语流畅，动作虽然古怪，但却很迅速。他的声音，抛去咳嗽的部分，也还正常。他能够表达复杂的思想。最重要的是，他似乎很有*活力*。

En: He is surprisingly okay.
Zh: 他的状态出奇地好。

En: It's a mystery. This animation comes at a cost too: erratic hand gestures, thought processes cut off like threads, as he just stares at the logs or the reeds. He also suffers mood swings, bubbling to the surface, unconstrained by his nervous system.
Zh: 这是一个谜。这种活力也是有代价的：古怪的手势，思维过程会像丝线一样被切断，只是呆呆地凝视着火堆或者芦苇丛。他的情绪非常不稳定，溢于言表，完全不受神经系统的控制。

En: Dementia?
Zh: 痴呆？

En: You've seen demented people before. This feels similar, yet different. When his thoughts move they are lucid, keen even -- not senile.
Zh: 你之前见过痴呆患者。这种感觉既熟悉，又很不同。当他的思想开始活动的时候，它们是明晰的，甚至是敏锐的——没有苍老衰弱。

En: And also flashes of lust, simmering in there. Not too common in people his age.
Zh: 还有欲望的闪现，叫嚣着即将爆发。对于他这个年纪的人并不常见。

En: Great leaps of emotion: from anger to grief, despair...
Zh: 情感的大跳跃：从愤怒到悲痛，再到绝望……

En: Mr. Dros, are you on some kind of a psychoactive substance?
Zh: “德罗斯先生，你在服用某种精神活性物质吗？”

En: Wouldn't a foul temper be a by-product of his life?
Zh: 坏脾气难道不就是他生活的副产品吗？

En: The lust. Wouldn't his *glands* have... stopped by now?
Zh: 欲望。他的*腺体*……现在不是应该停止了吗？

En: There is definitely something hormonal there. Whatever the condition he's suffering, there is a sexual element to it.
Zh: 这里肯定有些荷尔蒙的东西。不管他到底情况如何，肯定有一种性元素在里面。

En: No! he snaps out of staring through you. "I won't be stuffed full of shit like the rest of this city."
Zh: “不！”他的目光不再直透你的身体。“我不会像这座城市的其余部分一样，被这些狗屁玩意填满。”

En: In summary, you sense some underlying neurological disorder.
Zh: 概括起来，你感觉到某种潜在的神经障碍。

En: How about pyrholidon?
Zh: “那吡嚯浣酮呢？”

En: You said you take painkillers... The lieutenant follows your lead.
Zh: “你说过你在服用止痛药……”警督顺着你的思路往下走。

En: I take them to cope with pain. The people of this city use painkillers, because they have *pain*. Untreated illnesses, not enough money for a greedy doctor.
Zh: “我吃药是用来止痛的。这座城市的人使用止痛药，是因为他们*痛苦*。未经治疗的疾病，对于一个贪婪的医生来说，这点钱是远远不够的。”

En: It's not a downer. Rather an upper, judging by his snaps.
Zh: 不是镇定剂。从他的状态来看，更像是兴奋剂。

En: I don't know what that is... He shakes his head.
Zh: “我不知道那是什么……”他摇摇头。

En: He is indeed very lucid at times..
Zh: 他有时的确很清醒。

En: Are you sure about him being lucid? Maybe he's just affected by an extreme case of *socialism*.
Zh: 你确定他是清醒的吗？也许他只是被*工人主义*的极端情况影响了。

En: Are you on amphetamines?
Zh: “你在服用安非他命吗？”

En: Like some kind of decadent rock star? He doesn't dignify it with more of an answer.
Zh: “就像那些堕落的摇滚明星？”他并没有给出答案。

En: Mr. Dros, are you okay? How is your memory? (Finish the examination.)
Zh: “德罗斯先生，你还好吗？你的记忆怎么样？”（完成检查。）

En: Didn't he say...
Zh: 他不是说……

En: You said you take painkillers...
Zh: “你说过你在服用止痛药……”

En: No matter.
Zh: 没关系。

En: No I'm not *okay*... He waves his hand, chasing something that's not there. "I shit blood and I'm surrounded by insane people..."
Zh: 他摆摆手，追逐着某种并不存在的东西。“不，我不*好*，我便血，而且周围都是疯子……”

En: There it is again -- erratic hand motions. Bouts of rage. And the stomach thing too of course...
Zh: 又来了——古怪的手势。愤怒发作。当然了，还有胃部的问题……

En: Could it be a symptom of overdosing on something? Something even you have not tried? Keep your eyes peeled.
Zh: 有没有可能是服药过量的症状？甚至连你都没尝试过的东西？保持警惕吧。

En: Imbeciles... The old man in the dirty tracksuit trousers stares at you, panting from hatred. A gust of wind blows in from the sea, bending the reeds behind him...
Zh: “低能……”穿着脏运动裤的老人盯着你，因为憎恨而喘息着。海面吹来一阵风，压弯了他身后的芦苇丛……

En: Yeah, wait. We have more serious things to discuss.
Zh: “是啊，等等。我们还有更严肃的问题要讨论。”

En: The familiar *put put put* of hatred.
Zh: 那熟悉的*噗噗噗*憎恨。

En: Specifically, the whole city is a charnel house, stripped clean and draped in neon. But Martinaise... he shakes his head in grave disgust, "Martinaise is the worst."
Zh: “很明确，整座城市就是一座骸骨教堂，被剥的干干净净，挂满霓虹灯。不过马丁内斯……”他摇摇头，一副厌恶至极的表情，“马丁内斯是最糟糕的。”

En: Yes, the fly larva in his container. He let some nihilistic advertising yuppies erect a statue of Filippe III -- a syphilitic murderer -- on the town square. To spit on the working class.
Zh: “是的，蜷缩在集装箱里的那只苍蝇幼虫。他让一些虚无主义的广告业雅皮士竖起了一座菲利普三世的雕像——那个感染梅毒的杀人犯——就立在广场中央。来唾弃工人阶级。”

En: Not since the serfs of ancient Perikarnassis has History produced a more *inert* social class than the Martinaise proletariat. The rest of Revachol at least *pretends* to rebuild, these people still live in ruins...
Zh: “自从远古佩里卡纳西斯的农奴时代以来，历史还没有制造出比马丁内斯的工人阶级更加*呆滞*的社会阶级。瑞瓦肖别的地方至少还在*假装*重建，但这些人依然生活在废墟里……”

En: Anything more?
Zh: “还有别的吗？”

En: Let him finish, do not interrupt.
Zh: 让他说完，不要打断。

En: The worst of them is the blood-drenched *soucriant* on her yacht, licking her lips. The old whore's gone now, her gun toting porcelain men are dead -- so, actually, no...
Zh: “他们之中最糟糕的就是那个鲜血淋漓的*邪恶女巫*，乘着她的游艇，舔弄着嘴唇。现在那个老婊子已经离开了，她那个持枪行凶的陶瓷士兵也死了——所以，其实，不对……”

En: That all?
Zh: “就这些？”

En: Nod.
Zh: 点点头。

En: The *worst* is that old cock parading around in his uniform, throwing balls all day. It's not enough that the racists and liberals are dancing on our graves! The old loyalist ghouls still parade the ruins too.
Zh: “最*糟糕的*就是那只穿着制服趾高气扬的大公鸡，整天只知道扔球。种族主义者和自由主义者站在我们的坟头舞蹈还不够吗！那个老保皇派的食尸鬼也在废墟中游行。”

En: Detest pushes the cobwebs out of his eyes, pushes the melancholy aside. He relishes it.
Zh: 憎恨赶走了他眼中的混乱，把忧郁也赶在了一边。他感到十分享受。

En: In tents, like animals... He points to the church. "Like those boom-boom-morons on the ice. A pity they didn't drown in that tent of theirs." He keeps shaking his head with sorrow for the sight he missed.
Zh: “在帐篷里，像动物一样……”他指向教堂。“就像冰面上那群吵吵闹闹的白痴！可惜，他们没有淹死在自己的帐篷里。”他不停地摇着头，因为错过的景象而忧伤。

En: Every morning he's there. While the parasites he fought to protect are off in Ozonne, or Croyant-Morain, or some other island they've built their palaces on. Feeding on drugs and having sex with their own children.
Zh: “每天早晨他都会去那里。他保护的那群寄生虫还远远地待在欧佐纳尔，或者克里杨特-莫兰，或者其他造好宫殿的小岛上。以毒品为生，跟自己的亲生子女乱伦。”

En: That's all the rich *really* want -- sex with their own children. Throughout history -- even the royal bloodline of the Suzerain! --  it's all just an excuse for them to have sordid sex. At least that old cunt Frissel is now dead...
Zh: “这就是所有有钱人*真正*想要的——跟自己的亲生子女乱伦。在整个历史上——甚至是宗主国的皇家血统！——全都是他们用来做爱的借口。现在，至少那个老淫棍弗里塞尔已经死了……”

En: Just nod.
Zh: 只是点点头。

En: We did good when we pushed him under that horse car. If only... in the Thirties, those *disco whores*... What follows is ominous mumbling. You cannot make out a single word.
Zh: “我们把他推下马车的时候，其实做得很好。如果……能在30年代，那群*迪斯科婊子*……”接下来的只剩下一堆不祥的呓语。你一个字也听不出来。

En: The disco whores are too much; hatred shuts down his brain's language centre, leaving only a nonsensical sputter.
Zh: 迪斯科婊子有点过了；憎恨关闭了他大脑的语言中枢，只留下一堆毫无意义的飞溅唾沫。

En: Disco... whores?
Zh: “迪斯科……婊子？”

En: Hmmm... and the racist on the gates, pumped full of steroids?
Zh: “嗯……还有大门附近的种族主义者，身体里充满了类固醇？”

En: There was something about the statue on the roundabout -- and syphilis?
Zh: “环岛的那座雕像有点不对劲——还有梅毒？”

En: You mentioned the Union is social-democratic -- and Mr. Claire a farce of a social-democrat.
Zh: “你提到工会是工人主义派——还说克莱尔先生是工人主义的闹剧。”

En: The *boom-boom-boom morons* on the ice...
Zh: “冰面上那群*吵吵闹闹的白痴*……”

En: The blood-drenched soucriant on her boat -- what is a soucriant?
Zh: “船上那个鲜血淋漓的邪恶女巫——邪恶女巫是什么？”

En: By the 'cock parading in his colourful uniform,' you mean *René*?
Zh: “你说那个‘穿着彩色制服趾高气扬的大公鸡’，是指*雷内*吗？”

En: I'm glad we talked about this. Now...
Zh: “很高兴我们能聊聊这些事。现在……”

En: Glad? It's all gone... He stares into the dust at his feet. Grey and dirty spring grass all around.
Zh: “高兴了吗？全都没了……”他凝视着脚下的灰尘。周围都是会灰扑扑、脏兮兮的春草。

En: Mhm. Now -- I have some questions about this...
Zh: “嗯。现在——我有些问题……”

En: We did good when we pushed him under the horse car. Until, in the Thirties, those disco whores... He breathes in, his breath heavy with hatred, you cannot make out a single word.
Zh: “我们把他推下马车的时候，其实做得很好。直到30年代，那群迪斯科婊子……”他深吸一口气，呼吸中浓浓的都是敌意，你一个字也辨认不出来。

En: A strange little engine seems to fire up in him again. It straightens his back.
Zh: 他身体那个奇怪的小引擎好像又被点燃了，让他挺直了腰背。

En: How come?
Zh: “怎么会这样？”

En: Because of the *racists*. Everyone is a racist in Martinaise, it's their favourite thing to do in the whole world -- listening to race-themed radio shows. In the ruins, in their lorries. He points inland.
Zh: “因为*种族主义者*。马丁内斯的每个人都是种族主义者，在这个世界上，他们最喜欢的东西——就是收听种族主题的广播节目。在废墟里，在他们的卡车上。”他指向内陆。

En: Pumped full of steroids and Radio Revachol '92. Race this, race that... It's all sanctioned by that social democratic Union and the farce of a social democrat who runs it...
Zh: “满满的都是类固醇和瑞瓦肖92电台。种族这个，种族那个的……全都是那个工人主义的工会批准的，还有领导它的那个工人主义闹剧……”

En: Mr. Claire?
Zh: “克莱尔先生？”

En: Parody of a social democrat?
Zh: “工人主义的拙劣模仿？”

En: Whores... is all he says. Even that word has to be pushed through his teeth with great force. The rage seethes too hard.
Zh: “婊子……”他只说了这些。就算是那个词，他也是花了大力气从牙齿缝里挤出来的。怒火实在是烧得太猛烈了。

En: Yes -- *steroids*. A racist on steroids.
Zh: “是的——*类固醇*。一个打了类固醇的种族主义者。”

En: No, he answers for you. "The rich man's looking at real estate and fiddling his kids. Wading through the corpses..."
Zh: “不，”他替你做出回答。“有钱人只会看房地产，还有摆弄他的小孩。在尸体中跋涉前行……”

En: You're right. I too am a level headed moral person, unaffected by Measurehead.
Zh: “你说得对。我也是一个头脑冷静又有道德的人，没有受到测颅先生的影响。”

En: I for one *do* have an opinion on Measurehead that people need to hear. And it's not positive.
Zh: “就我个人而言，*的确*对测颅先生有点意见，需要让大家听到。而且一点也不积极。”

En: Did you notice that he is not... (Point toward the general area of your face.)
Zh: “你有没有注意到他不……”（指向自己脸部的大致区域。）

En: You mean Measurehead, right? What is your opinion on him?
Zh: “你是说测颅先生，对吗？你对他有什么意见？”

En: Got it.
Zh: “明白。”

En: What is my *opinion*? You think the rich man has an opinion on this idiocy? Is the rich man on the radio, telling you his opinion?
Zh: “我有什么*意见*？你觉得有钱人对这个白痴有意见吗？收音机里的那些有钱人，把意见告诉你了吗？”

En: Steroids and radio shows... His voice trails off into a despondent sputter. "Probably that Revachol 98AM that's *irradiating* the lumpen, it's the worst..."
Zh: “类固醇和广播节目……”他的声音逐渐消失，变成沮丧的低吟。“也许瑞瓦肖98AM*辐射*了那些流浪汉，这才是最糟糕的……”

En: I find it strange that you keep bringing this up. You moralist types are eternally fascinated with these race topics. Helping the ruling class conduct their swindle.
Zh: “很奇怪，你老是不停地提起这件事。像你这种道德家总是痴迷于这些种族主义的话题。帮助统治阶级进行诈骗。”

En: Old? An alcoholic? With each word he squints a bit more. "Fascist?"
Zh: “老？是个酒鬼？”每说出一个词，他的目光就越来越歪斜。“法西斯主义者？”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: No. I meant... (Keep pointing to your face.) "Generally reddish and pink, like me."
Zh: “不。我是说……”（继续指着你的脸。）“通常略带点红色还有粉色，像我一样。”

En: You're obsessed with your own features, he shakes his head, "because it's all you have left. The rich man took everything from you. Except your bottle."
Zh: “你被自己的容貌困扰了，”他摇摇头，“因为这就是你仅剩的一切。有钱人从你那里夺走了一切。除了你的酒瓶。”

En: And he left you here -- gesturing at your swollen face. The rich man rules over you and over the world. And he laughs at your fidgeting. He stares into the dead embers.
Zh: “然后他把你留在这里——对着自己肿胀的脸一通比划。有钱人统治着你和这个世界。而且他还在嘲笑你的坐立不安。”他凝视着余烬。

En: Not everyone is. He nods. "Most people are *young* and that. The rich man took everything from you, lumpenproletariat. Except your bottle..."
Zh: “并不是每个人都这样。”他点点头。“大部分都很*年轻*之类的。有钱人从你那里夺走了一切。除了你的酒瓶……”

En: The rich man doesn't care about *complexion*. He rules the world. And he laughs at your moral soliloquies. He stares into the dead embers.
Zh: “有钱人不在乎*肤色*。他统治着整个世界，而且还会嘲笑你的道德独白。”他凝视着余烬。

En: Then why was he *there*?
Zh: “那他为什么会在*那里*？”

En: Scratching away at the race question... You Moralintern monkeys keep the conversation going on Channel 4, helping the rich man conduct his swindle with this inanity.
Zh: “剖析种族问题……你们这群国际道德伦理委员会的猴子，一直在4频道继续这类话题，帮助有钱人用这些浅薄的话语骗人。”

En: Ha, yes, but he was *there*. Why was he *there* if you can't have an opinion?!
Zh: 哈，是的，不过他去过*那里*。如果你不能有意见的话，为什么他还会在*那里*？！

En: You think the rich man cares about your little race games? He rules the whole world. And he laughs at your moral soliloquies. He stares into the dead embers.
Zh: “你觉得有钱人会在乎你的小种族游戏？他统治着整个世界。他在嘲笑你的道德独白。”他凝视着余烬。

En: You moralist types are all cryptofascists, eternally fascinated with lurid race topics. Helping the ruling class conduct their swindle, keeping the inane conversation going on Channel 4.
Zh: “你们这种道德家都是秘密法西斯主义者，总是对耸人听闻的种族主义话题乐此不疲。帮助统治阶级进行诈骗，一直在4频道播放那些虚无空洞的对话。”

En: And your race garbage. You can keep that too, keep the conversation going for the rich man, on Channel 4, eroding working class solidarity. He stares into the dead embers.
Zh: “还有你那套垃圾种族主义理论。那个你也可以自己留着。为了有钱人在4频道不断播放那些对话，腐蚀工人阶级的团结性。”他凝视着余烬。

En: Now wait a moment. Let's get serious here.
Zh: 现在先等一下。让我们认真一点。

En: Let's get serious for a moment. This is the *real world* -- and in the real world it *means* something if your skin is a certain colour and you say things.
Zh: “我们稍微认真一下。这个*真实的世界*——在这个真实的世界，你的肤色还有你的话语都有着某种*意味*。”

En: Yeah no. Enough of that, this isn't going anywhere.
Zh: 别了。那个说得差不多了，这样没什么结果的。

En: By the way, I have internalized his *weltanschauung*.
Zh: “顺便说一句，我已经把他的*世界观*内在化了。”

En: I've seen the *real world*. In '06. The flags unfolding. Young people marching, being kind to each other. They dreamt of a million years in the stars. This here... He looks down at the ashes. "Is pale in waiting."
Zh: “我见过*真实的世界*。在06年。旗帜招展。年轻人游行示威，彼此友好相待。他们梦想着在星空中翱翔一百万年。而这里……”他低头看着灰烬。“只有灰域在等待。”

En: Oh, I'm sure you have *all* internalized your fair share of *racism* at the RCM. You don't have to tell me. It shows. Can't stop talking about some kind of head-measuring man...
Zh: “哦，我肯定你在RCM已经*完全*把你那部分的*种族主义*内在化了。你不用告诉我。它都表现出来了。控制不住地说些颅骨测量师的话题……”

En: The lieutenant gives you a nod. Go on, he seems to say.
Zh: 警督朝你点点头，似乎在说，继续吧。

En: Keep talking about the head-measuring man. Politics yields information from this man, gets him spilling facts.
Zh: 继续谈论颅骨测量师的话题。政治话题能从这个男人的嘴里套出信息来，让他无意中泄露事实。

En: The end. He nods. "The pale will swallow all of this. The water, the sky, the city. Soon. There is nothing to be serious about." He shakes his head. "We're all gone."
Zh: “结束了。”他点点头。“灰域会吞没所有一切。海水，天空，城市。在不久的将来。没什么需要认真对待的。”他摇摇头。“我们都会消失。”

En: Got it.
Zh: “明白。”

En: Pale in waiting?
Zh: “灰域在等待？”

En: They were boom boom imbeciles -- at least they're not there anymore. He gestures toward the ice. "What more is there to say? They're duped -- by bourgeois culture. I've seen their kind pop up, like worms after the rain."
Zh: “他们是一群吵吵闹闹的低能儿——至少现在已经不在那里了。”他朝着冰面那边比了个手势。“还有什么可说的？他们都被骗了——被资产阶级文化骗了。我见过他们那种人，不断地冒出来，像下雨之后钻出来的蠕虫。”

En: They're morons, duped by bourgeois culture. What *more* is there to say? I've seen their kind -- pop up, like worms after the rain. He makes an erratic gesture of annoyance.
Zh: “他们是一群白痴，被资产阶级文化骗的团团转。*还有*什么可说的？我见过那种人——不断冒出来，像下雨之后钻出来的蠕虫。”他做出一个烦扰的古怪手势。

En: Your beloved *Joyce Messier*. A soucriant. A blood sucker. Women like her *feed* on the life energy of young working class men. And they *let* her.
Zh: “你那位亲爱的*乔伊斯·梅西耶*。她是一个邪恶女巫。一个吸血鬼。那种女人是依靠工人阶级年轻小伙子的生命力*为食*的。而且他们*允许*她这样做。”

En: A soucriant, a blood-sucker, the rich whore on her boat.
Zh: “一个邪恶女巫，一个吸血鬼，就是船上那个有钱的婊子。”

En: Oh, you mean Joyce.
Zh: “哦，你是说乔伊斯。”

En: Oh, you mean the company representative.
Zh: “哦，你是说公司代表。”

En: Women like her *feed* on the life energy of young working class men. He nods. "And they *let* her...."
Zh: 他点点头。“那种女人是依靠工人阶级年轻小伙子的生命力*为食*的。而且他们*允许*她这样做。”

En: Your beloved *representative of the harbour company* is a soucriant. I saw her. Women like her *feed* on the life energy of young working class men. And they *let* her.
Zh: “你那位亲爱的*港口公司代表*是一个邪恶女巫。我看见她了。那种女人是依靠工人阶级年轻小伙子的生命力为食的。而且他们*允许*她这样做。”

En: He *could* be referring to Joyce in her youth. They must have listened to disco when they came here... but it's hard to say.
Zh: 他*可能*指的是年轻时的乔伊斯。他们来这里的时候肯定在听迪斯科……不过很难说。

En: Or maybe it's just his body and mind giving up on him, at last...
Zh: 或者，也许只是他的身体和思想终于放弃他了……

En: Syphilis is a disease Filippe III contracted in a whore house, he fires quickly. "The statue is an abomination."
Zh: “菲利普二世在一个婊子的家里感染了梅毒，”他噼里啪啦一股脑说了出来。“那座雕像令人厌恶。”

En: The bacteria entered his brain and made him squander trillions on sparkling wine, *cocainum*, and monuments of himself. His son Filippe IV the Insane contracted syphilis in the womb... he breathes in with a wheeze of hatred.
Zh: “细菌进入了他的大脑，让他在起泡酒，*可卡因*还有自己的纪念碑上浪费了数以万亿的财富。他的儿子，疯子菲利普三世还在子宫里的时候就染上了梅毒……”他愤怒地喘息着。

En: Abomination?
Zh: “令人厌恶？”

En: Good. Now that that's settled...
Zh: “好吧。既然那个已经说定了……”

En: Mhm, he sputters attentively.
Zh: “嗯，”他专注地吐着唾沫。

En: And he *still* went on to govern Revachol for twenty five years! We lost 2 million lives toppling that mode of government -- and those grotesque statues too, hundreds of them...
Zh: “而且，他*依然*继续统治了瑞瓦肖25年！为了推翻他的政府，我们失去了200万条性命——还有那几百个奇形怪状的雕像……”

En: But it's still there.
Zh: “不过它还在那里。”

En: What a keen remark! He sputters. "Yes it is, isn't it?! It's still there. Do you know *why*?"
Zh: “多么尖锐的评价啊！”他争论到。“的确如此，不是吗？它还在那里。你知道*为什么*吗？”

En: That is technically possible, although Filippe III was not actually syphilitic, he was just mad.
Zh: 从理论上来讲是可能的，只是菲利普三世实际上并没有感染梅毒，他只是疯了。

En: Because you forgot to take it down?
Zh: “因为忘记把它推倒了？”

En: Because cynical advertising yuppies erected a *deconstructed* version of it.
Zh: “因为愤世嫉俗的广告业雅皮士竖立了一个*解构*的版本。”

En: I don't know.
Zh: “我不知道。”

En: Because the king is holy and his statues are indestructible.
Zh: “因为国王是神圣的，他的雕像是坚不可摧的。”

En: Say: "Because the king is holy and his statues are indestructible." (But mean it ironically.)
Zh: 说：“因为国王是神圣的，他的雕像是坚不可摧的。”（其实是在讽刺。）

En: Forgot?! he spreads his arms. "The Party had each of the 141 Filippian monuments detonated with plastic explosives. We were *pedantic* about carrying out the order. No..." He points toward the city.
Zh: “忘记？！”他张开双臂。“党派用塑胶炸药引爆了141座菲利普的纪念碑。我们在执行命令这方面是很*迂腐*的。不是……”他指向城市。

En: Some advertising cockroaches used their accumulated capital to erect a new, ironic version of it. He shakes his head in disgust: "Art is a bourgeois establishment. It's an affront to humanity."
Zh: “一群广告业的蟑螂用他们积累的资本建造了一个新的、讽刺的版本。”他厌恶地摇摇头。“艺术是资产阶级的产物。这是对人类的一种冒犯。”

En: Wait, the bullet hole in Filippe's heart! The statue had been shot -- that was him!
Zh: 等等，菲利普雕像心脏上的那个弹孔！有人朝雕像开过枪——是他做的！

En: It had to be, he's already proven capable of taking a far more complicated shot from here.
Zh: 一定是这样，他已经证明自己拥有在这里进行更复杂射击操作的能力。

En: The bullet hole in Filippe's heart... he'd been shot in the heart. By him?
Zh: 菲利普雕像心脏上的弹孔……他的心脏中枪了。是他做的吗？

En: Actually, it was not deconstructed so much as *captured in the moment of the explosion*.
Zh: “事实上，与其说是解构，不如说*被爆炸的瞬间捕获了*。”

En: Actually, it was not de-constructed so much as *captured in the moment of the explosion*.
Zh: “事实上，与其说是解构，不如说*被爆炸的瞬间捕获了*。”

En: It was you. You shot the statue -- there was a bullet hole in his heart...
Zh: “是你。你打中了雕像——他的心脏上有个弹孔……”

En: That's it for the statue then. Tell me, Mr. Dros...
Zh: “雕像的事就说到这里。告诉我，德罗斯先生……”

En: Forget about that! Something much more noteworthy goes off in your brain! Could it not have been that...
Zh: 忘了那个吧！你脑袋里还有些更值得注意的东西！难道不是这样吗……

En: What are you talking about? The ancient communard cocks his head.
Zh: “你在说什么？”古老的公社社员昂起了头。

En: It's not a monument to Filippe III anymore -- it's a monument to the monument of Filippe III exploding.
Zh: “它不再是菲利普三世的一座纪念碑了——它是为了纪念菲利普三世爆炸的纪念碑。”

En: What is this madness? A dim light comes on in his eyes...
Zh: “你在发什么疯？”他的眼睛里闪烁着一道灰暗的光芒……

En: It's not madness. It's a monument to what you did. To your program of de-statuing Revachol.
Zh: “这不是发疯。这是你的一件丰碑。为了你的瑞瓦肖去雕像化计划。”

En: So you're saying it's... a *communist* monument now?
Zh: “所以你是说，现在它变成了一座……*康米主义*的纪念碑？”

En: Yes, and furthermore -- the design bureau people are probably left wing too. They often are.
Zh: “是的，此外——设计局的人也可能是左翼分子。一般来说都是。”

En: The king was a congenitally deformed adulterer. The Party had his monuments detonated with plastic explosives. That one was *especially* destructible... He points toward the city.
Zh: “国王是个天生畸形的通奸者。党派用塑胶炸药引爆了他的纪念碑。那一个*特别*容易破坏……”他指向城市。

En: That's right. Some *advertising* cockroaches erected a cynical, *deconstruction* of it. We tore it down with honest working class plastic explosives -- and there it is again... He shakes his head in disgust.
Zh: “没错。某些*广告业*的蟑螂创建了一个愤世嫉俗的*解构*版。我们用老实的工人阶级塑胶炸药把它拆掉了——结果它又立起来了……”他厌恶地摇摇头。

En: Until some *advertising* cockroaches used their accumulated capital to erect a new, ironic version of it. He shakes his head in disgust: "Art is a bourgeois establishment. It's an affront to humanity."
Zh: “直到某些*广告业*的蟑螂用他们积累的资本建造了一个新的、讽刺的版本。”他厌恶地摇摇头。“艺术是资产阶级的产物。这是对人类的一种冒犯。”

En: Art is a bourgeois establishment. It's an affront to humanity. Every gallery should be bulldozed and the artists should all be given 30 years of hard labour in Yekokataa.
Zh: “艺术是资产阶级的产物。这是对人类的一种冒犯。每个画廊都应该被拆掉，所有艺术家都应该去叶科卡塔做30年苦工。”

En: No, cynical *design* cockroaches like you erected a new, ironic version of it. We tore it down with honest working class plastic explosives -- but there it is again, grinning... He shakes his head in disgust.
Zh: “不，像你这样愤世嫉俗的*设计业*蟑螂建造了一个新的、讽刺的版本。诚实的工人阶级用塑胶炸药把它拆掉了——结果它又立起来了，咧开嘴笑着……”他厌恶地摇摇头。

En: I do. Some *advertising* cockroaches used their accumulated capital to erect a new, ironic version of it. We tore it down with honest working class plastic explosives -- but there it is again, grinning... He shakes his head in disgust.
Zh: “是的。某些*广告业*的蟑螂创建了一个愤世嫉俗的*解构*版。诚实的工人阶级用塑胶炸药把它拆掉了——结果它又立起来了，咧开嘴笑着……”他厌恶地摇摇头。

En: Wait! It suddenly strikes you... perhaps it was not as it seemed?
Zh: 等等！你突然想到……也许并不像看起来的那样？

En: Never mind.
Zh: “没事了。”

En: Delusional art bourgeois... He waves you off.
Zh: “妄想的艺术资产阶级分子……”他挥手让你离开。

En: The lieutenant, too, has cocked his head and is looking at you with a strange expression.
Zh: 警督也一样抬起头，带着一副奇怪的表情看着你。

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: Not only.
Zh: 不仅仅是这样。

En: I don't know. Maybe it's too far fetched.
Zh: “我不知道。也许有点太牵强了。”

En: We did always have the prettiest posters... Maybe you're right. His eyes fill with understanding. "That *is* how dialectics work. But understand this -- art is *still* a bourgeois institution."
Zh: “我们的海报总是最漂亮的……也许你是对的。”他的眼睛里充满了理解。“辩证法*就是*这么运作的。不过要理解一点——艺术*仍然*是资产阶级的产物。”

En: Maybe you're right... His dark eyes fill with the sudden understanding. "That *is* how dialectics work. But understand this -- art is *still* a bourgeois institution."
Zh: “也许你是对的……”突然间，他的黑眼睛里充满了理解。“辩证法*就是*这么运作的。不过要理解一点——艺术*仍然*是资产阶级的产物。”

En: He coughs, then adds, in a gravelly voice. "And they should all still be sent to Yekokataa."
Zh: 他咳了几声，然后以一种沙哑的声音补充到。“而且，他们还是应该被送去叶科卡塔。”

En: Of course it is. Delusional bourgeois musings. Everything is some kind of *subversion* on the way to the bank. He waves you off. "All artists -- on a train to Yekokataa."
Zh: “当然是了。妄想的资产阶级冥想。在通往金钱的道路上，所有的一切都是某种*颠覆*。”他挥手让你离开。“所有艺术家——都要乘上开往叶科卡塔的列车。”

En: Of course. Straight to Yekokataa.
Zh: “当然。直达叶科卡塔。”

En: I don't know about that. Could we not?
Zh: “那我就不知道了。可以不这么做吗？”

En: You know, you don't sound like a liberast anymore. Maybe I was wrong... He measures you with his black eyes. "Maybe I will be escorted to prison by another Mazovian communist -- as I should."
Zh: “你知道吗，你听起来已经不像个自由主义者了。也许我错了……”他用黑色的眼睛打量着你。“也许我应该被另一个马佐夫康米主义者送进监狱——理应如此。”

En: Yes, well... the ancient communard sizes you both up, not sure what to think of it.
Zh: “是的，好吧……”古老的公社社员打量着你们俩，不确定该怎么想。

En: Typical far left treachery, repressing your own people.
Zh: “典型的极左派背叛行为，压迫你们自己人。”

En: It is *commonplace* to relocate the workforce according to the needs of the land. All nations do it. It's called settlement. He shakes his head and mumbles: "Browbeating from the bourgeois art-fascist..."
Zh: “根据对土地的需求重新安置劳动力是*司空见惯*的事情。所有国家都应该这样做。这叫移民。”他摇摇头，嘟囔到：“来自资产阶级艺术法西斯分子的威胁……”

En: Straight to Yekokataa for this old revisionist. He gives you a little nod. "At last -- atonement for my sins: revisionism, reactionary ideation, desertion..."
Zh: 他朝你微微点点头，表示赞同。“为这个老修正主义者直接送到叶科卡塔。最后——为了弥补我的罪恶：修正主义，反动观念的形成，逃亡……”

En: As you ought to. *Communism* style.
Zh: “你应当这么做。按照*康米主义者*的风格。”

En: You're wrong. I'm no communard. I'm a cop.
Zh: “你错了。我不是公社社员。我是一名警察。”

En: For a middle-class cop you're damn good at getting information out of an old revisionist deserter. Go ahead, he gives you an acknowledging little nod.
Zh: “对于一个资产阶级警察来说，你很擅长从一个老修正主义逃兵那里挖出信息。来吧，”他朝你微微点点头，表示赞同。

En: He still refuses to believe you subscribe to Mazovian socio-economics, but entertaining the thought has given him some measure of solace.
Zh: 他依然拒绝相信你赞同马佐夫社会经济学，不过这种愉悦的思想还是给了他一些安慰。

En: Camaraderie even. For a second there he feels less alone -- but then the second passes.
Zh: 甚至可以说是同伴之间的友情。有那么一两秒，他感觉没那么孤独了——不过这一刻转瞬即逝。
